channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - New York Knicks
channel.description: New York Knicks trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: OG Anunoby expected to be ready for Game 1 of Eastern Conference Finals
item.description: Knicks Videos: "OG Anunoby will play in Game 1. I feel very comfortable saying that, just because of where this thing was in the day or two days after it happened." On The Putback with @IanBegley , Ian says that barring any setbacks, the Knicks should expect to have OG Anunoby ready to go for Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Finals

item.title: Front office maintaining asking price of "a young ‚Ä¶
item.description: Underdog NBA: Latest on Giannis Antetokounmpo trade front, per @ShamsCharania : - Front office maintaining asking price of "a young blue-chip talent and/or a surplus of draft picks" - Bucks also leaving the door open to pursue a major trade to convince Giannis to stay once again - Wolves, Cavs, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers pursued Giannis at the trade deadline

item.title: The altar was here. The casket was there. There were ‚Ä¶
item.description: In her dining room, Kathleen Reinhard can still see the tall teenager at her son‚Äôs funeral service in 2012. She is wearing a dark New York Knicks hoodie while sitting near a tray of her homemade banana bread, describing the church scene by pointing to different spots on her table. The altar was here. The casket was there. There were rows of pews here and here, and Karl-Anthony Towns was right over there. Reinhard didn‚Äôt know who Towns was on that winter morning inside St. Agnes Church in Clark,

item.title: ‚ÄúFor all the accomplishments I had, it meant nothing ‚Ä¶
item.description: Towns was also a St. Joseph of Metuchen class president who thought he should be present for a fallen alum, a 25-year-old member of the United States Marine Corps who was among six killed in a helicopter crash while supporting combat operations in Afghanistan. Kevin Reinhard was once a strong 6-foot-4 volleyball player on a powerhouse St. Joe‚Äôs team, and Towns was overwhelmed by the outpouring of affection and respect for him. At 16, he was already used to being celebrated in print. ‚ÄúI grew up i

item.title: ‚ÄúThey‚Äôre screaming for me to shoot,‚Äù he recalled. But ‚Ä¶
item.description: Towns had to do something, anything, to honor a Marine he had never met. So the following day, he scored 25 varsity points against Perth Amboy ‚Äî one for every year Reinhard spent on the planet ‚Äî and then shut it down. ‚ÄúI just stopped shooting,‚Äù Towns recalled. His grandmother lived in Perth Amboy, and his entire family was in the stands, wanting him to add on to his new career high. ‚ÄúThey‚Äôre screaming for me to shoot,‚Äù he recalled. But Towns was done looking at the basket, he said, ‚Äúbecause I ha

item.title: ‚ÄúIt was the darkest time of my life when Kevin was ‚Ä¶
item.description: Another 6-foot-10 St. Joe‚Äôs player, James Ziemba, recalled being stunned by his younger friend‚Äôs maturity and awareness. Towns had quietly laid out his plan before tipoff. ‚ÄúIt‚Äôs a nice thing to want to do,‚Äù Ziemba said, ‚Äúbut then to actually do it, as a freshman? ‚ÄúThat was Karl. He always wanted to be part of that community. It wasn‚Äôt just a place for him to pass through and move on.‚Äù The Reinhard family wasn‚Äôt aware of this small act of kindness until they talked to school officials about start

item.title: Timberwolves, Cavaliers, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers could pursue Giannis Antetokounmpo depending on how their playoffs go
item.description: The NBA playoffs represent another landmark point for the Antetokounmpo sweepstakes. Sources said postseason teams such as the Minnesota Timberwolves, Cleveland Cavaliers, Boston Celtics, Knicks and Los Angeles Lakers pursued Antetokounmpo at the February deadline, and their finishes to the campaign will play a major factor in their aggressiveness to trade for Antetokounmpo.

item.title: Largest point differential during a seven-game win ‚Ä¶
item.description: ‚Ä™Tommy Beer‚Ä¨: Largest point differential during a seven-game win streak in the playoffs in NBA history: 1. 2026 Knicks: +185 . . . . . . . . . . . . 2. 1985 Lakers: +133 3. 2017 Cavs: +131 4. 2010 Magic: +129 5. 2026 Thunder: +128 6. 1986 Celtics: +127

item.title: The New York Knicks have outscored their first and ‚Ä¶
item.description: ‚Ä™NBA Stat‚Ä¨: So far in the 2026 NBA Playoffs, the New York Knicks have outscored their first and second round opponents by 194¬Ýpoints, the largest margin in league history over any 10-game postseason span. Previous mark of 171 points was set by the Golden State Warriors in 2017. (via @statitudes.com ) #NBA

item.title: Josh Hart: I used to think Philly was a sports town, I don't know if it is anymore
item.description: [SNY] Josh Hart says he wasn't surprised at how many Knicks fans were in Philly: "I used to think Philly was a sports town, I don't know if it is anymore"

item.title: It's significantly cheaper to go down to Philly and ‚Ä¶
item.description: Yaron Weitzman: It's the prices. Or, more specifically, how insanely expensive it is to go to MSG. That's it. It's significantly cheaper to go down to Philly and watch a game than to go the Garden. For Lots of Knicks fans, this is the only way they can even dream of going to a Knicks playoff game.

item.title: Tyrese Maxey on Knicks fans taking over the 76ers arena: ‚ÄòIt absolutely sucks‚Äô
item.description: Derek Bodner: Tyrese Maxey on the Knicks fan takeover in Philly: "It absolutely sucks. It just sucks. That's really all I can say about it, man...There's only one way to put a stop to it: we have to go out there and win these games. It felt louder here for them than it did in the Garden."

item.title: Joel Embiid, on the Knicks: "At times, it's okay to ‚Ä¶
item.description: Derek Bodner: Joel Embiid, on the Knicks: "At times, it's okay to just say the other team was better....Gotta get better, from top to bottom. Ownership, front office, players, coaches. Everybody just gotta get better."

item.title: Miles McBride on reaching back-to-back Eastern ‚Ä¶
item.description: Ian Begley: Miles McBride on reaching back-to-back Eastern Conference Finals: ‚ÄúThat‚Äôs what‚Äôs expected‚Ä¶ that‚Äôs not the goal.‚Äù

item.title: The Knicks are the 4th team in NBA history with ‚Ä¶
item.description: ESPN Insights: The Knicks are the 4th team in NBA history with multiple 30-point series-clinching wins in a single postseason. They join the 2025 Thunder, 2008 Celtics, and 1987 Lakers. All 3 of those teams went on to win the NBA title.

item.title: Knicks sweep Philadelphia in lights-out shooting game
item.description: Tim Bontemps: Final: Knicks 144, Sixers 114 An absolute demolition by the Knicks, who sweep Philly, pick up a seventh consecutive victory overall & advance to the Eastern Conference finals for a second straight year. Philly has not advanced to the conference finals for a quarter century.

item.description: Stefan Bondy: Knicks just tied the NBA playoff record for most 3-pointers in a game.

item.title: Best Knicks playoff series ever in point differential: ‚Ä¶
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Best Knicks playoff series ever in point differential: 2026 vs. Atlanta: +105. 2026 vs. Philadelphia: +89. Yes, their two best marks in NBA history have happened in this postseason

item.title: Knicks will sweep a best-of-7 series for the first ‚Ä¶
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Knicks will sweep a best-of-7 series for the first time since 1999 & the third time in team history. Last time the Knicks swept a series, they went to the Finals...

item.title: The Knicks will now have at least a week off until the ‚Ä¶
item.description: Adam Zagoria: The Knicks will now have at least a week off until the conference finals. The earliest they would play Game 1 would be next Sunday, per Mike Breen.

item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Knicks with 18 threes in the first half, tied for the most in a playoff half in NBA history. Most since the Cavs in 2016.

item.description: ESPN Insights: The Knicks are rolling early. They have tied the NBA playoff record for most 3-pt FG in any quarter (11).

item.title: This crowd is overwhelmingly -- I mean overwhemingly ‚Ä¶
item.description: Stefan Bondy: This crowd is overwhelmingly -- I mean overwhemingly -- Knicks fans. It's really like a game at MSG.

item.title: OG Anunoby out vs. Sixers
item.description: The Knicks are playing it cautiously. OG Anunoby (right hamstring strain) is out for their Game 4 clash against the 76ers Sunday afternoon at Xfinity Mobile Arena as they look to¬Ýfinish off a series sweep. He also missed the Knicks‚Äô Game 3 win on Friday with the injury. He is still considered day-to-day and the Knicks have been optimistic that he won‚Äôt miss much time. Imaging revealed that¬Ýit is a ‚Äúvery minor‚Äù strain, The Post‚Äôs Stefan Bondy previously reported.

item.title: Prior to the playoffs, the most-watched game of the ‚Ä¶
item.description: While some of the credit may belong to Anthony Edwards and the Timberwolves ‚Äî who have played in highly-viewed games in each of their past two playoff runs ‚Äî the Spurs account for several high water marks this season. Prior to the playoffs, the most-watched game of the season was Spurs-Thunder on ABC/ESPN Christmas Day and the most-watched outside of broadcast television was the Knicks-Spurs NBA Cup Final on Prime.

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns, an avid card collector, revealed ‚Ä¶
item.description: Karl-Anthony Towns, an avid card collector, revealed that he was outbid for a one-of-one Randy Orton WrestleMania 41 patch autograph. The winning bid came in at $42,100 on eBay. ‚ÄúThis has us SICK,‚Äù Towns wrote on his Instagram account ‚Äúbigbodegascards,‚Äù which is dedicated to his card collecting. ‚ÄúThought it was ours for the taking, until a last-minute NUCLEAR bid snuck in! Who has this card now!? We have to know!‚Äù

item.title: OG Anunoby 'a go' for East Finals
item.description: Shams Charania: The latest for @SportsCenter on optimism for New York's OG Anunoby entering the Eastern Conference finals and details on Bob Myers leading the search for the 76ers new top executive: ‚ÄúSources tell me that OG Anunoby, there's an expectation that he will be able to play in game one of the Eastern Conference Finals, barring any setbacks. There's optimism for his status for game one today, OG Anunoby participated in parts of practice. He also had an individual on court workout. He's

item.title: Jalen Brunson: We have our plan. Obviously utilize ‚Ä¶
item.description: Jalen Brunson: We have our plan. Obviously utilize rest, but then¬Ýwhen we're in the gym, no, we're¬Ýdoing everything we have to do to stay¬Ýin rhythm. Stay having the edge that¬Ýwe need to have. Focusing on the things that we need to¬Ýfocus on and continuing to prepare.¬ÝObviously, we have to prepare for um one¬Ýof these two teams and then¬Ýwe've talked about the differences¬Ýbetween the two and all that, but¬Ýjust focusing on what makes us the best¬Ýthing we can be as well.

item.title: I was blessed with it not¬Ýbeing a fracture and ‚Ä¶
item.description: You got hurt. Four guys got hurt. Do you¬Ýever let yourself think like maybe luck¬Ýis on our side this time? This wasn't as¬Ýbad as it felt. Josh Hart: No, I mean,¬Ýno, I don't really believe in the luck. For me¬Ýpersonally I pray before¬Ýevery game in terms of¬Ýprotection for myself, my team, and¬ÝI just go out there and hoop,¬Ýwhen I had the hand, I'm just I obviously walked back and got X-rays of that. I was just I'm¬Ýsitting there praying about it. I mean, that's my first go-to.¬Ýjust sitting there pra

item.title: Sixers owner on Knicks fans taking over Philly: We did everything we could, including buying tickets to keep them out
item.description: This was what happened in the 76ers-Knicks series, as¬ÝNew York¬Ýtook over Xfinity Mobile Arena in Game 4 with¬Ýchants of ‚ÄúKnicks in four‚Äù. This was jarring to¬Ýhear, as it did not seem like Philly was playing at home at all. 76ers owner¬ÝJosh Harris¬Ýmade his feelings¬Ýregarding the matter perfectly clear in a press conference on Thursday night. ‚ÄúObviously I didn‚Äôt like it. It‚Äôs our homecourt. We did everything we could, including buying tickets to keep the [Knicks] fans out. It‚Äôs on us. The fans supp

item.title: Sixers owner Josh Harris "Obviously I didn't like ‚Ä¶
item.description: New York Basketball: Q "How did it feel sitting‚Ä¶with all the Knicks fan that took over?" Sixers owner Josh Harris "Obviously I didn't like it‚Ä¶We did everything we could‚Ä¶to keep the fans out‚Ä¶Let's face it‚Äìwe got swept‚Äìso‚Ä¶there were some Knicks fans‚Ä¶Our job's to come back & win that series next year"

item.title: Knicks playoff run could generate $140 million for Madison Square Garden Sports
item.description: The¬ÝNew York Knicks¬Ýhave punched their ticket to the Eastern Conference finals for the second season in a row after last playing in the ECF in 2000, producing a windfall for¬ÝMadison Square Garden¬ÝSports, which owns the club and the NHL‚Äôs Rangers. The Knicks have already played five playoff home games, which generated nearly $50 million in gross revenue before the NBA took its cut from ticket receipts. The team is likely in line for at least five more home games, as the heavy favorite versus whoe

item.title: He estimates the Knicks earned $8 million per home ‚Ä¶
item.description: The Knicks generated $115 million from the¬Ýplayoffs¬Ýlast year,¬Ýaccording to¬ÝSeaport Research Partners analyst David Joyce, who covers the publicly traded MSGS. He estimates the Knicks earned $8 million per home game during the first round of the 2026 playoffs from tickets, suites, concessions and merchandise. It jumped to $12 million per game in the second round when the Knicks swept the Philadelphia 76ers.

item.title: Since the Game 4 adjustments against Atlanta, he‚Äôs ‚Ä¶
item.description: Since the Game 4 adjustments against Atlanta, he‚Äôs averaging 99 touches per 100 possessions,¬Ýaccording to¬ÝSecond Spectrum. Against the Sixers, that number climbed to 103. During the regular season, he averaged only 83. This is a different Karl-Anthony Towns, more in line with the one head coach Mike Brown tried to create early in the season but who could not get comfortable as the hopeful hub of the offense. Now, he‚Äôs adopted this style and been one of the best players in the NBA playoffs.

item.title: During the regular season, Towns took a shot on 26 ‚Ä¶
item.description: The shots are way down because Towns is choosing for them to be, not because the Knicks are failing to include him. He‚Äôs never received the basketball this much since joining the Knicks, and he‚Äôs revamped what he does next. During the regular season, Towns took a shot on 26 percent of his touches,¬Ýaccording to¬ÝSecond Spectrum. During the seven-game winning streak, that rate is down to 15 percent. He‚Äôs passing on 71 percent of his touches, compared to 61 percent during the regular season. In the

item.title: Aas he admitted, his head was fixated on being drafted ‚Ä¶
item.description: Aas he admitted, his head was fixated on being drafted by the New York Knicks. "All I was thinking about was New York," Curry said. "But careful what you wish for. I'm glad it went my way for sure." Curry added that his favorite part of the night was being booed by New York fans inside The Theater at Madison Square Garden in New York City. He remembers initially questioning why they were booing him, but then he realized it was because they wanted him on the Knicks.

item.title: "Everything he did today, we did" - Mike Brown
item.description: Knicks Videos: OG Anunoby fully participated in Knicks practice today "Everything he did today, we did" - Mike Brown

item.title: "I'm texting Ant, I'm texting Rudy, I'm texting those ‚Ä¶
item.description: Knicks Videos: Karl-Anthony Towns has been locked in on watching his former team, the Wolves, during the playoffs: "I'm texting Ant, I'm texting Rudy, I'm texting those guys"

item.title: "He's done a great job of adjusting our team to give ‚Ä¶
item.description: Knicks Videos: "He's done a great job of adjusting our team to give us the best chance to win. The spot we're in now is because of his courage and trust to change what we were doing and put us in a better position." - Karl-Anthony Towns on Mike Brown

item.title: "Go in there say how your feeling, with your feelings, ‚Ä¶
item.description: Stefan Bondy: KAT implies he brought idea of changing offense to Mike Brown before Game 4 against the Hawks. That's when the Knicks started running offense thru KAT in midpost: "Go in there say how your feeling, with your feelings, your idea, and see if it's received. And it was received."

item.title: Kings interested in Mitchell Robinson?
item.description: Still, sources around the league have pegged the Sacramento Kings as a threat to pry away Robinson in free agency. Scott Perry, the GM of the Kings, has long been a fan of Robinson after drafting him the 2018 second round as a member of New York‚Äôs front office. It was probably Perry‚Äôs best move in a tenure of blunders before Rose arrived. Perry‚Äôs Kings have no cap space but could maneuver the roster to open a competitive offer for Robinson, who has mostly been underpaid throughout his career ($6

item.title: The NBA Draft Lottery set a record last weekend, with ‚Ä¶
item.description: The NBA Draft Lottery set a record last weekend, with ABC drawing 2.3 million viewers. That led into the Knicks finishing a sweep of the 76ers in the Eastern Conference semifinals. ABC was up 23% from the lottery last year. USA Network averaged 529,000 viewers for its debut WNBA game on Sunday afternoon, featuring Aces-Sparks. The game on Mother‚Äôs Day was up 18% from the WNBA‚Äôs average on cable TV last season. Aces-Sparks also had the youngest median age of all national WNBA game broadcasts on o

item.title: Baron Davis: Jalen Brunson is Allen Iverson in this era
item.description: Baron Davis: Jalen Brunson to me is¬Ýlike Allan Iverson in this era.¬ÝLike nobody can guard him. No matter¬Ýwhat you do, he going to get his shot¬Ýoff. He gets hot. You know, it's on Fuego¬Ýand he can play to the moment.

item.title: OG Anunoby practiced again Saturday with the New York ‚Ä¶
item.description: OG Anunoby practiced again Saturday with the New York Knicks and said the right hamstring injury he sustained in these playoffs wasn‚Äôt as bad as one he suffered two years ago.

item.title: ‚ÄúIt‚Äôs always great when your coach has got trust in ‚Ä¶

item.title: Dubai BC interested in Guerschon Yabusele
item.description: Dubai BC is reportedly very interested in bringing Guerschon Yabusele back to Europe after his last two seasons in the NBA. The French forward played for the Philadelphia 76ers, New York Knicks and Chicago Bulls, confirming himself as an experienced and physical profile for the European market. Dubai‚Äôs interest would fit the club‚Äôs ambitious EuroLeague project.

item.title: MSG Sports to split Knicks and Rangers into two separate companies
item.description: Madison Square Garden Sports has moved to split the New York Knicks and New York Rangers into two separate, publicly traded companies ‚Äî a move analysts say could finally unlock billions in trapped franchise value tied up under James Dolan‚Äôs sprawling sports empire. MSG Sports said it confidentially filed an initial Form 10 registration statement with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Monday as it advances a proposed tax-free spin-off that would separate the Knicks business from the Range

item.title: MSG Sports, however, currently carries a public market ‚Ä¶
item.description: Forbes valued the Knicks at $9.75 billion last year, while CNBC recently pegged the franchise above the $10 billion mark amid soaring NBA media-rights expectations. The Rangers, meanwhile, were valued by Forbes at $4 billion ‚Äî making them the NHL‚Äôs second-most valuable franchise behind only the Toronto Maple Leafs. MSG Sports, however, currently carries a public market valuation of roughly $8.5 billion ‚Äî far below the combined estimated private-market value of the Knicks and Rangers. Investors h

item.title: ‚ÄúObviously, you'd rather not have a 9-day break ‚Äì ‚Ä¶
item.description: Josh Hart added that the break ‚Äì a roughly 218-hour interlude ‚Äì is something they would rather not have to deal with, but it did offer them a chance to reset. ‚ÄúObviously, you'd rather not have a 9-day break ‚Äì you're in a good rhythm, then you've kinda got to sit there and wait,‚Äù Hart said. ‚ÄúIdeally, if it was a three- or four-day break, that would've been nice. Good points and bad points [to the rest].‚Äù

item.title: ‚ÄúWe have to put pressure on them in all phases, ‚Ä¶

item.title: Should you want to sit more centrally ‚Äî albeit still ‚Ä¶
item.description: As the Knicks pursue their first NBA Finals berth since 1999, prices for in-person attendance at Madison Square Garden have surged. For proof, try looking from the top corner of the arena in Game 1 on Tuesday, a nosebleed view that would run you at least $570 before fees, according to StubHub. Should you want to sit more centrally ‚Äî albeit still in the nosebleeds ‚Äî you‚Äôre looking at over $700, and a seat closer to the lower bowl or courtside as Game 1 approaches could run you over $13,000. item.title: Investment in young players in 2022 weakly predicts ‚Ä¶
item.description: Claude did some statistical analysis, and found something interesting: the correlation strengthens over time. Investment in young players in 2022 weakly predicts wins by 2024 (r = 0.29), but very strongly predicts wins by 2026 (r = 0.66). What we see in these electric Western conference finals are teams that began playing young players years ago. The same is true of the playoffs generally. (The Knicks are a bit of an exception. 19th in the league in playing young players over the last four years

item.title: James Harden, Cavaliers interested in an extension, but his performance in Knicks series could affect his contract
item.description: How James Harden performs the rest of the series could affect his contract this summer. He has a player option for next season worth $42.3 million, but he was acquired with both the Cavs and Harden's camp interested in an extension, sources told ESPN.

item.description: Get-in prices for Games 3 and 4, as of Tuesday morning, are the highest in NBA history, according to one ticket outlet. The cheapest ticket for Game 3 at MSG on June 8 costs $3,410 at Gametime, at the time of writing. For Game 4 two days later, the cheapest ticket costs $3,242 at Vivid Seats. item.title: If the Finals reaches six games, fans would have to ‚Ä¶
item.description: If the Finals reaches six games, fans would have to fork up over $4,500 on June 16. It‚Äôs a stark contrast to 10 years ago when Cleveland Cavaliers and Golden State Warriors faced off in the 2016 NBA Finals and the average price to get into those games was $880. Last year, when the Thunder and Pacers clashed, the average ticket price was $1,147, according to TickPick.

item.title: Kenny Atkinson to remain as Cavaliers head coach
item.description: The Cavaliers plan to retain coach Kenny Atkinson even after the team was swept Monday night by the New York Knicks, two league sources told The Athletic. Atkinson guided the franchise to its first appearance in the conference finals since 2018 and first without LeBron James on the roster since 1992, but there was some question coming out of the series about potential changes Cleveland might make. Instead, no major changes to the front office or coaching staffs are now expected, per those source

I think Leon [Rose] and [William Wesley] did a great job putting together like-minded individuals. And that‚Äôs what it takes. Guys that are not gonna get too high, get too low, and they cheer for one another and they l

item.description: As these current Knicks finally brought the franchise back to the biggest stage, what advice does Starks have? ‚ÄúJust enjoy the moment, the most important thing that these guys do,‚Äù Starks told The Post. ‚ÄúYou never know when you‚Äôre gonna get back. When we lost the Finals in ‚Äô94, I thought we‚Äôd be back a couple of times, [which] didn‚Äôt happen for me and my teammates, for some of us. Just enjoy the moment.‚Äù

item.title: President Trump is considering making an appearance at ‚Ä¶
item.description: President Trump is considering making an appearance at the N.B.A. finals next week, a rarity for an American leader, after his hometown New York Knicks clinched a championship spot, according to three people familiar with the matter.

Because you want to give back. It's the feeling that somebody believes in you and that you're responsible to do a job. It makes you have that willingness to do it right, do it for the team in the right reasons, because you want to grow and you want to take those steps, whatever your goals are in your career. But I thi

item.title: Leon Rose puts his arm around his son, Sam, and ‚Ä¶
item.description: Leon Rose puts his arm around his son, Sam, and visibly breaks down in tears after the emotions set in of the Knicks going to the NBA Finals. This is beautiful stuff. We love you, Leon. Thank you for everything you have done.

item.title: Bulls to interview Johnnie Bryant
item.description: Marc J. Spears‚Ä¨: The Chicago Bulls have received permission to interview Cavaliers associate head coach Johnnie Bryant for their head coach opening, a source said. The former University of Utah guard has 12 years of NBA assistant coaching experience and has also been an associate head coach with the New York Knicks.

item.title: Donald Trump to attend NBA Finals game?
item.description: President Trump is considering making an appearance at the N.B.A. finals next week, a rarity for an American leader, after his hometown New York Knicks clinched a championship spot, according to three people familiar with the matter. An appearance by Mr. Trump at Madison Square Garden would be the latest incidence of a high-profile sports event serving as a backdrop for his presidency. Mr. Trump last year showed up at the Super Bowl, the Daytona 500, the U.S. Open men‚Äôs final and a Yankees game.

item.title: If the Knicks had lost Game 4 of the Eastern ‚Ä¶
item.description: If the Knicks had lost Game 4 of the Eastern Conference finals on Monday, Mr. Trump had planned to make a surprise appearance among the team‚Äôs faithful at Madison Square Garden on Wednesday, according to three people familiar with the planning. But after the Knicks completed a sweep of the Cleveland Cavaliers on Monday, the president is turning to the N.B.A. championship.

item.title: ‚ÄúI really like Jim Dolan a lot, I‚Äôm really happy for ‚Ä¶
item.description: Mr. Trump has said in recent weeks that he remains a Knicks fan and has been following the team‚Äôs progress. ‚ÄúI really like Jim Dolan a lot, I‚Äôm really happy for him and the team,‚Äù Mr. Trump said of James Dolan, the owner of the Knicks, the New York Rangers and Madison Square Garden, on WABC-AM, known as TalkRadio 77. ‚ÄúI think it‚Äôs great.‚Äù

item.title: Following Olympiacos' EuroLeague triumph on Sunday, ‚Ä¶
item.description: Following Olympiacos' EuroLeague triumph on Sunday, Nikos Tsakos welcomed the trophy to his company's offices during a shipping conference. There, he also made a surprising remark about the possibility of a friendly game between Olympiacos and the Knicks. "We support the team of our hometown and the city where we grew up. I was born in Piraeus and moved to New York at 17," he said, via Sport24. "We are happy that the two teams are among the most historic in the world. Soon we will be able to do

item.title: His son, Panagiotis Tsakos, went even further when ‚Ä¶
item.description: His son, Panagiotis Tsakos, went even further when discussing the idea. "It's something we are discussing with the team," he said regarding a potential exhibition game. "Either for the inauguration of the renewed stadium, or at Madison Square Garden. As you know, with half a million Greeks, we could fill the venue."

item.title: Donald Trump said he plans on attending an NBA Finals ‚Ä¶
item.description: Stefan Bondy: Donald Trump said he plans on attending an NBA Finals game after being invited by James Dolan

item.title: Donte DiVincenzo to return during the 2026-27 season?
item.description: It is far too early in the recovery process to predict when Donte DiVincenzo will return to action. Tearing an Achilles tendon is among the most serious injuries in the game, often keeping players out for a year or more. Boston‚Äôs Jayson Tatum returned in less than 10 months, but that is considered an outlier. Still, DiVincenzo is trying to follow Tatum‚Äôs lead. He flew to New York for surgery less than 24 hours after suffering the injury, following a similar timeline that Tatum went through. DiVi

item.description: Louis Williams: "I got to tell you Charles Oakley is he's a urban legend around here. I've heard stories about Oak. Man, give me something good. Give me something good about Oak, man." Derek Harper: "I got something for you. When I first got to New York, I had to be initiated, like we're in college or something. So Oak, (Anthony Mason), John (Starks), all of them. They had me on the elevator in Boston. He tells me, this is the best time for us to do it. I said, do what? Like, what you talking ab

item.title: NJ Transit
item.description: NJ Transit won‚Äôt change World Cup plans to give Knicks fans a direct ride to a historic playoff home game ‚Äî favoring soccer tourists over local sports lovers, officials said. New York City‚Äôs on-fire hometown team will host the NBA finals at Madison Square Garden for the first time in nearly 30 years ‚Äî potentially including a high-stakes Game 6 on June 16, the same day a match between France and Senegal is set to kick off at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, NJ.

item.title: Transit riders traveling to Madison Square Garden for ‚Ä¶
item.description: NJ Transit riders traveling to Madison Square Garden for the Knicks game at 8:30 p.m. June 16, against the Thunder or Spurs, will need to switch to PATH trains to get into Manhattan at the 33rd Street station, a spokesperson for NJ Transit said. Asked if the policy could be tweaked due to the potential World Cup-NBA Finals crossover, a spokesperson said no changes will be allowed.

item.title: They are donating hundreds of free tickets to ‚Ä¶
item.description: The Knicks are looking out for their fans. They are donating hundreds of free tickets to underprivileged youth for their home games in the Finals, The Post has learned. Underserved New York families affiliated with their Garden of Dreams Foundation will receive 250 tickets per home game. That means a guaranteed 500 tickets across Games 3 and 4. If there is a Game 6, that number will rise to 750.

item.title: Many claim they had been ruined by that ancient sports ‚Ä¶
item.description: Fans nonetheless gathered outside the arena on Monday night after the Knicks completed a sweep of the Cleveland Cavaliers. The police said that conditions were less raucous than they had been at the Knicks‚Äô most recent home game on Thursday, but there were six arrests, and fans scaled subway entrances, drank in public and blocked traffic. Longtime die-hards have rued the cancellation of the official watch parties. Many claim they had been ruined by that ancient sports enemy: fake fans ‚Äî posers ‚Äî

item.title: ‚ÄúEverybody just wants to get a viral clip out instead ‚Ä¶
item.description: Social media influencers have become bandwagon Knicks fans, capitalizing on how the formula has been shown to draw audiences. That, in turn, has led to grousing online. One Reddit user said last week that it felt like ‚Äúhalf of the people‚Äù outside the Garden were just there to mug for the cameras. ‚ÄúEverybody just wants to get a viral clip out instead of actually celebrating the win,‚Äù the user wrote, adding, ‚ÄúIt just feels fake.‚Äù

item.title: ‚ÄúFor sure. For sure,‚Äù McBride told The Post. ‚ÄúIt was ‚Ä¶
item.description: McBride, a second-round pick, averaged 2.2 points on 29.6 percent from the field in 40 games as a rookie. You wondered whether he‚Äôd ever make it in the NBA, and that skepticism remained for another one and a half seasons as McBride‚Äôs playing time/shooting efficiency hovered around unimpressive. Asked Monday to reflect on his personal journey to the Finals, McBride acknowledged, for the first time, that self-doubt crept in. ‚ÄúFor sure. For sure,‚Äù McBride told The Post. ‚ÄúIt was some tough, long nig

We went to Puerto Vallarta, Mexico. We went to St. Barts ‚Äî fantastic island, man, I can't wait to go back. ‚ÄúWe actually came to New York City, too, and ran around New York for like four days. We went to this one club and we met these young dudes that work on Wall Street, and I was there with them till like five in the morning. They sent me some pi

item.description: ‚ÄúBut, you know, I just wanted to have fun, and if an opportunity came up, great,‚Äù he added. ‚ÄúIf it didn't, you know, shoot, I felt lucky, blessed, fortunate. I had a good run, you know? And I felt that at some point, I'd get another opportunity, whether it was a head coach or an assistant coaching position. So I just kind of rolled with it. Didn't think much about it. And, you know, obviously, this opportunity came up. You know, from afar, I just felt that this team was ready. And I'm just thril

item.title: Steve Kerr, Gregg Popovich ‚Äî they were guys that went ‚Ä¶
item.description: ‚ÄúYou know, Steve Kerr, Gregg Popovich ‚Äî they were guys that went deep into their bench, and they both always used to say, ‚ÄòIt's not about now, it's about the postseason,‚Äô‚Äù Brown said after Game 3. ‚ÄúYou know, you keep guys engaged by doing that, and you do develop not just the bench, but the team as well, because guys get used to playing with other guys, just in case someone goes down.‚Äù

item.title: "They have some great players. I think I'll be going ‚Ä¶
item.description: FoxNews: NEW: President Trump reveals he was invited to a New York Knicks game for their first NBA finals appearance in 27 years: "I was invited... I was going to go on Wednesday, but they closed it out very quickly." "They have some great players. I think I'll be going to one of the games. I was invited by, numerous people." "The Knicks have really... they really suffered for years".

item.title: ‚ÄúI ask him to name the starting lineup from the 1993 ‚Ä¶
item.description: FoxNews: Kathy Hochul attempted to dunk on President Trump over the Knicks ‚Äî and ended up getting roasted by fans instead. The New York governor tried to quiz the president's support for the team when asked about him potentially attending an NBA Finals game. ‚ÄúI ask him to name the starting lineup from the 1993 championship team and see how he does,‚Äù Hochul said. There‚Äôs just one problem: the Knicks didn‚Äôt win a championship in 1993. Their last NBA title came in 1973.

item.title: "You have to bring him back," an East¬Ýexecutive¬Ýsaid. ‚Ä¶

"You have to bring him back," an East¬Ýexecutive¬Ýsaid. "If they win the Finals, they're not losing anybody. And, even if they don't, I don't see how they let him walk."

Right before the playoffs, however, Anunoby underwent an emergency appendectomy, forcing him to miss the entire playoff run that culminated in the Raptors winning their fi

He can think about it. He probably won‚Äôt, though. Not yet. ‚ÄúWhen I took the job, I thought about it,‚Äù Brown said during a Zoom conference call with reporters on Sunday. ‚ÄúBut going through this process, one of the things that I‚Äôm trying re

item.title: "Just worry about what we have to do to be better. Not ‚Ä¶

Not get too into the media and all the stuff. A lot of questions, a lot of talk about how great we are, how great we‚Äôve been. That doesn‚Äôt matter, we just gotta worry about being ourselves and stay locked in. And go win."

item.title: Mitchell Robinson suffered a broken pinky; no timetable for return
item.description: Knicks center Mitchell Robinson broke his right pinky finger earlier this week, and there is no timetable for his return, league sources told The Athletic.

item.description: In addition to his work for ESPN, Breen has called Knicks games locally for over 30 years. He began as the team‚Äôs radio announcer in 1991, and after a brief stint as the lead play-by-play man later that decade, he took over the position for good in 2004. ‚ÄúIt‚Äôs not just that I was a Knick fan since I was a kid, but I‚Äôve been broadcasting Knick games for over half my life,‚Äù Breen said. ‚ÄúSo, you know, when you see a team have a chance to do something they haven‚Äôt done forever, there‚Äôs certainly emo

item.description: The cheapest resale tickets for the next game in that building would soon be available for the preposterous, plausible price of more than $3,000 apiece. Of course, New York has many teams in many sports that stir many unruly passions. Fans of Rangers hockey are avowed lunatics, but have a smaller footprint in a city where a public park‚Äôs netless hoop is something of an unofficial municipal logo. item.title: Mitchell Robinson plans to play in Game 1
item.description: Shams Charania: Mitchell Robinson has undergone surgery on his broken right pinky finger -- and fully plans to play in the New York Knicks' Game 1 of the NBA Finals against OKC or San Antonio next Wednesday while wearing a brace on his hand, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: No definitive timetable on Mitchell Robinson‚Äôs return
item.description: Ian Begley: There is still no definitive timetable on Mitchell Robinson‚Äôs return but there is hope within Knicks that he can be back for NBA Finals, per league sources. NBA Finals start on Wednesday. As noted earlier, Robinson will push hard to return to play in time for Finals.

item.title: The timing of the injury & surgery is key here. Since ‚Ä¶
item.description: Jeff Stotts: Latest Mitchell Robinson Injury Update: The timing of the injury & surgery is key here. Since 2005, the quickest return from a pinkie fracture that needed surgery in the NBA is 14 days. Sounds like Mitch is going to try to beat those numbers.

item.title: ‚ÄúAll I see this morning is anger at me for not having ‚Ä¶

And during the recent episode of his self-titled show, KOC apologized to Knicks fans for putting a small blot on Brunson‚Äôs otherwise spectacular season. ‚ÄúAll I see this morning is anger at me for not having Jalen Brunson on one of my three All-NBA teams, and I am shocked I was the only voter that didn‚Äôt have him on one of the teams,‚Äù said O‚ÄôConnor on his show. ‚ÄúSo I‚Äôm here to say I‚Äôm sorry to Knicks fans.‚Äù

item.description: NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani says he's down to watch the Knicks in the NBA Finals, but don't expect to see him sitting courtside with President Donald Trump ... that's IF the commander-in-chief shows up at Madison Square Garden. Jacob caught up with Mamdani Thursday in Queens, and asked him about Trump saying he plans to attend a Finals game after being invited by Knicks owner James Dolan. Mamdani doesn't sound too interested in making it a bipartisan basketball outing ... saying, "If the president

item.title: The mayor sounded fired up about the Knicks' playoff ‚Ä¶
item.description: The mayor sounded fired up about the Knicks' playoff run, though ... joking he hopes the Western Conference finalists "keep going double OT" so whoever survives comes into the Finals exhausted.

item.title: Mitchell Robinson did not sustain his broken pinky in ‚Ä¶
item.description: Fred Katz: Mitchell Robinson did not sustain his broken pinky in a game or in practice, Mike Brown said. After a follow-up question to Brown about how Robinson actually got hurt, Knicks media relations stepped in to say, "We're not going to get into specifics."

item.title: Mike Breen on Knicks: 'I‚Äôm so happy for the fan base because there were so many nights where they were dreadful'

Remember that the Knicks asked for permission to speak to Jason Kidd, Ime Udoka, Quinn Snyder, Chris Finch, and Billy Donovan. All of those teams said no. Jason Kidd and Billy Donovan both got contract extensions and no longer coach the teams where they were a year ago. item.title: The backup center suffered a fracture of his fifth ‚Ä¶
item.description: Mitchell Robinson‚Äôs specific injury is identified. The backup center suffered a fracture of his fifth metacarpal, league sources told The Post, which is the bone connecting the wrist to the pinky finger.

item.title: Recovery is often measured in weeks following surgery ‚Ä¶
item.description: Jeff Stotts: ANOTHER Mitchell Robinson Injury Update: The injury is now believed to be a 5th metacarpal fx, meaning it's more of a hand injury than a finger one. Recovery is often measured in weeks following surgery (AVG = 52 days) but it still sounds like Mitchell is going to push to play.

item.title: ‚ÄúThe ones that want to see me down and hurt all I ‚Ä¶
item.description: The Knicks big man took to Instagram on Saturday, days after it was revealed he broke his pinky in the lead-up to the NBA Finals. ‚ÄúI can‚Äôt thank you guys enough for the love and support most of you bring especially at a time like this in my life. It makes everything I‚Äôm fighting for 100x easier to deal with,‚Äù he wrote, without specifically mentioning the pinky injury.‚Äù However, his haters got a much different message. ‚ÄúThe ones that want to see me down and hurt all I gotta say for you is f‚Äìk you

item.title: NBA will have a different NBA champion for 8th ‚Ä¶
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: The NBA will have a different NBA champion for 8th straight season, extending the longest streak in league history. 2019 - Toronto 2020 - Los Angeles Lakers 2021 - Milwaukee 2022 - Golden State 2023 - Denver 2024 - Boston 2025 - Oklahoma City 2026 - New York/San Antonio

item.title: Jeremy Sochan set to receive 2026 NBA ring regardless of Finals outcome
item.description: New York Knicks¬Ýforward¬ÝJeremy Sochan¬Ýis already set to receive a 2026 NBA championship ring before the Finals even begin. Regardless of whether the Knicks or the San Antonio Spurs win the series, Sochan is expected to receive the reward. Typically, NBA championship rings are awarded not only to players on the Finals-winning team, but also to any players who appeared for that franchise during the regular season. Sochan started the season with the Spurs, appearing in 28 games before being waived.

item.title: Green doubles down that the Knicks need a true 1A ‚Ä¶
item.description: NBA Courtside: Draymond Green doubles down that the Knicks need a true 1A player to win a championship: ‚ÄúJust like Becky Hammon said, prove me wrong. Absolutely double down. Getting out of the East has never been a sure fire thing to winning a championship. What y‚Äôall talking about? You‚Äôre supposed to get out of the East. It‚Äôs the f*cking East!‚Äù

item.title: Fans hoping to attend Game 1 of the NBA Finals between ‚Ä¶
item.description: Fans hoping to attend Game 1 of the NBA Finals between the San Antonio Spurs and New York Knicks will need deep pockets, with resale ticket prices topping $1,200.

item.title: The cheapest options were located in the arena's ‚Ä¶
item.description: According to a Ticketmaster listing, the lowest available resale tickets were listed at approximately $1,245 per seat as of Sunday. The cheapest options were located in the arena's upper-level seating sections, while premium seats were listed for more than $4,300 each before taxes.

item.title: Isaiah Hartenstein on Knicks: 'I'm just rooting for them right now'
item.description: Justin Martinez: Isaiah Hartenstein on his former team, New York, competing in the NBA Finals: "Happy for them. ... I'd rather be playing against them, but I'm just rooting for them right now. There's a lot of guys I know. There's a lot of guys I'm pretty close with. So just happy for them."

item.title: Josh Hart on how he reacts to centers ‚Äî and, more ‚Ä¶
item.description: Fred Katz: Josh Hart on how he reacts to centers ‚Äî and, more specifically, Victor Wembanyama ‚Äî guarding him so they can roam into the lane: "The only unanimous Defensive Player of the Year is guarding me, so that's a good sign for me, right? ... I'm a pretty good player."

item.title: Deuce McBride on the Spurs' young guards: "They're ‚Ä¶
item.description: Fred Katz: Deuce McBride on the Spurs' young guards: "They're relentless. They're young. ... (They) just go out there and make it a tough game. I love that."

item.title: Mitchell Robinson practices individually, status for Finals Game 1 still TBD
item.description: Ian Begley: Mike Brown said Mitchell Robinson did individual stuff in practice today. Obviously, he is playing with his hand protected. Brown did not have a Game 1 status for Robinson. Robinson will certainly push to play in the game

item.title: The Brunsons will become the first father-son duo to ‚Ä¶
item.description: NBA Communications: Rick Brunson played for the Knicks in the 1999 NBA Finals. Jalen Brunson will do the same in 2026. The Brunsons will become the first father-son duo to each play in the NBA Finals for the same franchise. Game 1: Wednesday, June 3 at 8:30 PM ET on ABC.

item.title: Josh Hart, one of the smartest players in the league, ‚Ä¶
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Josh Hart, one of the smartest players in the league, was asked today at Knicks practice about what the team can learn from playing the Spurs in the NBA Cup final. "Technically, that game didn't happen," Hart said. (It didn't count in season standings or stats, so he's right.)

item.title: Mike Brown said Mitchell Robinson has to be cleared by ‚Ä¶
item.description: Stefan Bondy: Mike Brown said Mitchell Robinson has to be cleared by the medical staff before "he can go out there."

item.title: NBA Finals court designs will feature the Larry O'Brien Trophy
item.description: Matt Guzman: Here's a look at the courts both teams will use for the NBA Finals, affixed with the Larry O'Brien Trophy at the center. This looks awesome. #PorVida

item.title: For one bettor on DraftKings Sportsbook, however, a ‚Ä¶
item.description: The Knicks finally know who their opponent will be following Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs‚Äô monumental Game 7 closeout win over the Thunder on Saturday night. For one bettor on DraftKings Sportsbook, however, a $49,000 straight bet on the Knicks to win the NBA Finals for a massive $980,000 payout still hangs in the balance. The finals winner bet was made a day after the Knicks beat the Hawks in Round 1 on May 1, as they were priced with 19-to-1 odds to win the championship.

item.title: ‚ÄúI mean, that was December. I don't think that's ‚Ä¶
item.description: Josh Hart, in fact, took the comedic route in explaining why there's nothing for the Knicks to glean from that game. ‚ÄúI mean, that was December. I don't think that's really gonna be equivalent to what the atmosphere and energy is going to be like,‚Äù Hart said, via SNY. ‚ÄúTechnically, that game didn't happen, so I don't think there's anything that we can learn from.‚Äù

item.title: He got rid of a lot of the people that had connections ‚Ä¶
item.description: Brian Berger on Blazers owner Tom Dundon: "He's been a bull in a china shop so far. He's come in and just cut costs. He didn't allow t-shirts. I look at teams like the Knicks and the Spurs and you see all the former players sitting courtside and how the teams have embraced the former players. He got rid of a lot of the people that had connections to the former players last week when they laid off 70 people. He's cutting hotel costs. He's running this kind of like he runs the Carolina Hurricanes,

item.title: He agreed to a four-year deal with the Magic that ‚Ä¶
item.description: Sweeney, 41, will continue with the Spurs through the NBA Finals, the Magic said. San Antonio has homecourt advantage when it takes on the New York Knicks in the Finals, which start Wednesday night on ABC. He agreed to a four-year deal with the Magic that includes an additional one-year team option, a league source confirmed to the Orlando Sentinel. Sports Illustrated first reported the length of Sweeney‚Äôs contract.

item.title: Jeremy Sochan on how to get Victor Wembanyama tired: Give him different looks, be very physical and make him run
item.description: Jeremy Sochan signed with the Knicks after clearing waivers, and now his new team will face off against his old team in the NBA Finals. ‚ÄúWhether I‚Äôm playing or not, it‚Äôs important to feed all the information I have. And I think I know quite a lot,‚Äù Sochan said in an exclusive interview with Jared Schwartz of the New York Post. ‚ÄúBeing that tall [Wembanyama] gets tired,‚Äù Sochan continued. ‚ÄúGive him different looks and [not beat him up but] be very physical, and make him run. And he‚Äôs gonna get tir

item.title: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is making sure that New York City ‚Ä¶
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is making sure that New York City schools students can stay up late to watch the Knicks play in the NBA Finals. The mayor was joined by a group of students Monday morning to sign an executive order that repeals bedtime so the kids can stay up late to watch the Knicks. "Bedtime is repealed! All of you can watch the Finals," the mayor declared.

item.title: Big Perk expects the Spurs-Knicks series to go 7 games ‚Ä¶
item.description: ESPN: Big Perk expects the Spurs-Knicks series to go 7 games üî•üèÄ ‚ÄúI expect another seven game series. This is David and Goliath.‚Äù

item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is making sure that New York City schools students can stay up late to watch the Knicks play in the NBA Finals. The mayor was joined by a group of students Monday morning to sign an executive order that repeals bedtime so the kids can stay up late to watch the Knicks.

item.description: SNY Knicks: Mike Brown says Mitchell Robinson did individual work today and Brown is waiting on the Knicks' medical staff to tell him what the next steps are

item.title: According to EPM, Towns had the best defensive season ‚Ä¶
item.description: But is the newfound elite defensive performance sustainable? Listen to enough NBA podcasts, and you‚Äôll frequently hear the take that the Knicks have two massive defensive holes to patch: Brunson at the point and Karl-Anthony Towns in the frontcourt. So even with a trio of very good ‚Äúwing-stopper‚Äù defenders in OG Anunoby, Mikal Bridges and Josh Hart, they‚Äôre sort of treading water. The critique of Brunson is fair. He‚Äôs not a black hole like Trae Young out there, and he gets his share of steals, b

item.title: There‚Äôs probably been some luck on offense too ‚Äî the ‚Ä¶
item.description: There‚Äôs probably been some luck on offense too ‚Äî the Knicks are shooting an even 40 percent from three in the playoffs, up from 37.3 percent in the regular season. Still, offense and defense aren‚Äôt as separable in basketball as is commonly assumed. The Knicks are scoring a spectacular 1.32 points per possession in transition in the playoffs; they actually aren‚Äôt getting that many steals, but the ones they do get, largely from their wing defenders, have often been pick-sixes at midcourt that set

item.title: The coaches were thinking about how he would fit in, ‚Ä¶
item.description: Jusuf Nurkic: The bubble was the hardest and the best experience in the world, probably the best basketball ever played ‚Äî it was just basketball, food, and sleep. We got tested every day, and Carmelo figured out that if he didn‚Äôt take the test, he had 24 hours free. We would all be practicing, and then wonder how he got a day off while we were working. He wouldn‚Äôt show up for testing and would get a day off. It was like elementary school, they would take you by the hand to go get tested. We woul

item.title: Perhaps that will happen again in the NBA Finals, ‚Ä¶
item.description: New York Knicks fans have infiltrated opposing arenas throughout their team‚Äôs storybook playoff run. With ticket prices at Madison Square Garden out of reach for many Knicks lifers, several have hit the road to get their postseason fix. Perhaps that will happen again in the NBA Finals, especially now that, according to Gametime data from Monday morning, the get-in cost for Games 1 and 2 in San Antonio is dropping while seats in MSG are growing increasingly expensive.

item.title: Jamal Crawford: That fanbase and the connection to the ‚Ä¶
item.description: Why does the city love the Knicks so much? Jamal Crawford: That fanbase and the connection to the fanbase, it‚Äôs one of the truly remarkable things in sports ‚Äî in any sport. They‚Äôre like how the Cowboys are loved, how the Yankees are loved, how the Lakers are loved. It‚Äôs a different type of love. Here‚Äôs an example: I came to a game a couple of years back, playoffs against Cleveland. And when I left, it was like I played and I had 30 in the Garden the night before, the fans were going crazy. I‚Äôm l

item.title: I think if guys can stay healthy during this Finals ‚Ä¶
item.description: Why do you believe the Knicks can win the championship? Marcus Camby: I just think they‚Äôre playing their best basketball right now. They‚Äôve been scoring at a high clip, shooting the ball at a high percentage, everything just seems to be clicking right now. ‚Ä¶ I think if guys can stay healthy during this Finals run, I think we‚Äôll have a real good shot at bringing the title home to New York.

item.title: What makes Jalen unique is for a guy his size, he can ‚Ä¶
item.description: What makes Jalen Brunson great? Marcus Camby: He‚Äôs been around pro athletes his whole life with his dad (Rick), a former teammate of mine, and a great coach, and he‚Äôs just been well-schooled from when he was a young guy. ‚Ä¶ What makes Jalen unique is for a guy his size, he can pretty much do it all. He can shoot it, he can drive it to the basket, he has a great, great low-post game for a guy his size and the body that he has, and he‚Äôs left-handed. I think that gives him an advantage as well. So h

item.title: They're really surprised that the Spurs are favored by ‚Ä¶
item.description: NBA Base: Brian Windhorst says people around the league are surprised by how much the Spurs are favored against the Knicks: "You know the more and more people I've talked to around the league Greeny, scouts and other coaches, they're really surprised that the Spurs are favored by as much as they are. They get with Wembanyama and how tough the West playoffs were, but they really believe that the Knicks have some advantages in the series and that this thing could absolutely go New York's way."

item.title: Jeremy Lin ‚Äî the Knicks guard who captivated the city ‚Ä¶
item.description: Linsanity will be part of the NBA Finals. Jeremy Lin ‚Äî the Knicks guard who captivated the city for about a month in February 2012 ‚Äî is joining ESPN during his former team‚Äôs NBA Finals matchup with the Spurs, USA Today reported. His role will start Wednesday with Game 1 in San Antonio.

item.title: Mamdani signed an executive order on Monday titled ‚Ä¶
item.description: Zohran Mamdani‚Äôs relentless quest to corner the youth vote has continued with the news that New York‚Äôs mayor has repealed bedtime for the city‚Äôs children during the hometown Knicks‚Äô NBA finals run. The Knicks are in the finals for the first time since 1999, and the series against the San Antonio Spurs starts on Wednesday. Mamdani signed an executive order on Monday titled ‚Äúrepealing kids‚Äô bedtimes for Knicks Finals run.‚Äù In words that will strike terror into the hearts of parents hoping to get t

item.title: Anonymous NBA scout picks Spurs over Knicks
item.description: Minutes after the Spurs' Game 7 victory in Oklahoma City, ESPN's Tim Bontemps spoke with a veteran NBA scout on the keys to the Finals: "I think I still give the Spurs the advantage, but there's a nagging thing from the end of this game: With all the emotion, it immediately hit me that they may have a hangover coming out of this series. The Knicks have the problem of being off for a long time, so you can say that's its own issue, but it just seems San Antonio put so much into this series ... hav

item.title: That is only the greatest 11-game stretch any team has ‚Ä¶
item.description: Now, led by Brunson -- who has already placed himself on the Mount Rushmore of all-time great Knicks alongside those three stars listed above -- New York comes roaring into the Finals having ripped off 11 straight victories with a point differential of plus-262. That is only the greatest 11-game stretch any team has played in the 80 years the NBA has existed, covering both regular and postseason play.

item.title: Remarkably, their offensive efficiency was the highest ‚Ä¶
item.description: The Knicks just finished smoking the Eastern Conference competition, posting a 123.3 offensive rating on their way to the NBA Finals. Remarkably, their offensive efficiency was the highest intraconference mark posted by an East champion since the league's current playoff format was instituted in 2003, supplanting LeBron James' 2017 Cleveland Cavaliers.

item.title: While the Spurs ranked third in defensive efficiency ‚Ä¶
item.description: Get this: The Knicks also posted a blistering 123.0 offensive rating in their two regular-season games against San Antonio, and they torched the Spurs in the NBA Cup championship (which doesn't officially count in the standings). While the Spurs ranked third in defensive efficiency during the regular season, they conceded more points per possession to the Knicks than any of their opponents. This Finals will be decided by San Antonio's ability -- or inability -- to slow the New York juggernaut. T

item.title: ‚Äú(If you could) go into a lab and create‚Ä¶the physical ‚Ä¶
item.description: Ian Begley: ‚Äú(If you could) go into a lab and create‚Ä¶the physical profile you need to have a chance against him, it probably would look a lot like OG Anunoby.‚Äù On NBA Finals on ABC conference call, analysts Tim Legler and Richard Jefferson were asked about OG Anunoby potentially defending Victor Wembanyama. Legler and Jefferson's full thoughts on Anunoby defending Wembayama: Legler: ‚ÄúJust looking at Wemby all year and sort of the evolution of his career‚Ä¶if you could have a physical profile that

item.title: "But for me, OG Anunoby - even the way that he's ‚Ä¶
item.description: "But for me, OG Anunoby - even the way that he's guarded him this year - is the type of defender that you have to have (in order to have) a chance. Because of (Anunoby‚Äôs) combination of just agility laterally, which you need - length to contest as much as you can from a guy that's 7-5 with a high release point, and then the physical strength and determination. You have to have a guy that is so committed to that end of the floor; and in this case, it's unique because (Anunoby) isn't a guy who's a

item.title: "So it's a major task for him and the minutes he's on ‚Ä¶
item.description: "So it's a major task for him and the minutes he's on the court; he'll be the primary guy on him. And I think in this situation the Knicks are probably going to do what they can to kind of keep him on (Wembanyama) as much as they can. Certainly, sometimes you have to switch or there‚Äôs a cross match. but he is the guy that they would want to have. I think they've got about as good a defender as you can for this type of matchup/responsibility.‚Äù

item.title: Would agree‚Ä¶. And understand, there is no stopping ‚Ä¶
item.description: JEFFERSON: "I would agree‚Ä¶. And understand, there is no stopping players in the NBA. That's not a real thing. I told a story a little bit ago about Kobe Bryant; (he) said Tony Allen was the best player to ever guard him. And Kobe averaged 29 (points per game) on Tony Allen. So while OG is a perfect player, in my opinion, to guard Wemby, the size, the consistency, the competitiveness in which he brings, he's still giving up a foot. So it's one of those things that, it's the physicality aspect, an

item.title: Victor Wembanyama on facing Knicks in the Finals: 'They're right where they're supposed to be'
item.description: Ian Begley: Victor Wembanyama on Knicks matchup: ‚ÄúIt‚Äôs a great team of experienced guys who are not here by chance‚Ä¶. They got here (through) relentless effort‚Ä¶ they‚Äôre right where they‚Äôre supposed to be.‚Äù

item.description: Ian Begley: Brooklyn native Julian Champagnie says he has a bunch of friends who are Knicks fans. He was asked if he‚Äôs prepared to be the villain in his hometown: ‚ÄúSure‚Ä¶I would love to spoil their (plans),‚Äù Champagnie said with a laugh today at NBA Finals media day

item.description: Jared Schwartz: Mitchell Robinson arrives for Knicks practice. Nothing on his right hand as he walked in

item.title: UConn coach Dan Hurley rooting for Spurs despite being a Knicks fan: I'm rolling with Stephon Castle
item.description: Dan Hurley has been a Knicks fan all his life. Favorite player: Bernard King. But this isn‚Äôt about a Jersey City guy and a love affair with the darlings of Madison Square Garden. This is about UConn basketball family, now. The Knicks may be in the NBA Finals for the first time since 1999, may be going for their first championship since 1973, but they‚Äôd have to beat Stephon Castle to do it. ‚ÄúNo, I‚Äôm not torn,‚Äù Hurley said told The Courant on Tuesday morning. ‚ÄúI mean, I love the Knicks. They have

item.title: ‚ÄúOne of the more appealing Finals we‚Äôve seen,‚Äù Hurley ‚Ä¶
item.description: ‚ÄúOne of the more appealing Finals we‚Äôve seen,‚Äù Hurley said. ‚ÄúYou‚Äôve got the Knicks and the history of the Knicks, a more veteran group, versus this young and talented, ahead-of-schedule Spurs team.‚Äù

item.title: Tre Donaldson has completed NBA pre-draft workouts ‚Ä¶
item.description: Tobias Bass: Miami‚Äôs Tre Donaldson has completed NBA pre-draft workouts with the Jazz, Suns, Spurs, Lakers, Warriors, Clippers, Knicks He will also workout with the Hornets, Bucks, Heat, Kings, Raptors, Celtics, Pistons, Magic, Nuggets and Trail Blazers

item.title: The Spurs announced a lineup of free fan events and ‚Ä¶
item.description: The Spurs announced a lineup of free fan events and activations ahead of their NBA Finals matchup against the New York Knicks, including a downtown pep rally, watch parties and special River Walk art installations. The team's traditional Finals pep rally will be held tonight at the Red McCombs Community Court at Hemisfair at 801 Cesar Chavez Blvd.

item.title: The watch parties at Madison Square Garden are coming ‚Ä¶
item.description: The watch parties at Madison Square Garden are coming back for the NBA Finals, the team announced Tuesday. The world‚Äôs most famous arena will host a watch party for Game 1 against the San Antonio Spurs at 8:30 p.m. Wednesday. The doors open at 7:30 p.m., and the $10 tickets were sold out within an hour on Ticketmaster. There will also be a watch party at SummerStage in Central Park for Game 1 on Wednesday night, officials said. The event is free, but registration is required.

item.title: Josh Hart on meeting Jalen Brunson at Villanova: 'I thought he was one of those annoying five-star recruits'
item.description: NBA: "I hated Jalen. I thought he was one of those annoying 5-star recruits... Mikal was the same way, I hated him too." Josh Hart on his first impressions of Jalen Brunson and Mikal Bridges during their time at Villanova

I mean, it's his team. He's the boss. He's not, I guess, overbearing or around all the time. When he is, we don't always talk about basketball, we talk about other things, too. ‚Äú

item.title: Mike Brown on James Dolan: ‚ÄúHe challenges Leon [Rose]. ‚Ä¶
item.description: Ian Begley: Mike Brown on James Dolan: ‚ÄúHe challenges Leon [Rose]. He challenges me. He challenges the players in the right way. You want that from your boss. You want to be challenged, you want to be pushed. There's a way to do it, there's a way not to do it. My short time around him, he's been fantastic in that area. In the same breath, too, he's adamant if you bring to him what you need, then there's backing with it, he's adamant about trying to give you the best things possible to go get the

Brown said he‚Äôs going to talk to NYK medical staff to find out what Robinson can do in practice today. Robinson has done individual work in practice since hand injury. No contact yet.

item.description: Oh No He Didn't: OG on being back in the Finals, only this time playing in it: "The first time it was really but it wasn't the way I would have wanted it. So this time just happy to be here, happy to be healthy"

"My stepmom worked at the Westin, so I would see the parades through the hotel balconies," he said. "I would sneak around there at the hotel, with (the opposing teams) staying there, being able to take pictures and run up on players for autographs. I was definitely that kid, seeing that energy and how alive this city comes when th

item.title: Mitchell Robinson listed as questionable for Game 1
item.description: Ian Begley: Mitchell Robinson is questionable for Game 1 of Finals tomorrow due to a fractured right 5th Metacarpal. Nothing has changed surrounding the injury. He'll push to play & NYK remains hopeful he'll be out there. Robinson practiced with team, iced his hand afterward

item.title: Mitch Johnson said Fox‚Äôs sore right ankle is trending ‚Ä¶
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Spurs have no injuries to report ahead of Game 1. Mitch Johnson said Fox‚Äôs sore right ankle is trending toward 100%.

item.title: James Dolan on why Knicks chose Mike Brown: We wanted someone who was collaborative and flexible
item.description: What convinced you that Mike Brown was the right man for this job? James Dolan: Leon (Rose) convinced me (laugh). When we knew we were gonna make the change, we didn‚Äôt tell the players about making a change. We first had talked to all the players and I was there, and then sat down with Leon, and we talked about what it is we want in a coach. ‚ÄôCause we knew we had a good coach in Thibs. We weren‚Äôt just going for a change, right? There was something we wanted, and we laid it out, really on paper,

item.title: I did not go into the interview thinking, ‚ÄúOh I‚Äôm ‚Ä¶
item.description: What compelled you to issue your NBA Finals-or-Bust proclamation in January? James Dolan: That‚Äôs why I don‚Äôt do a lot of interviews ‚Äî I‚Äôm usually very frank (smile). I did not go into the interview thinking, ‚ÄúOh I‚Äôm gonna say this.‚Äù But it was on my mind, and I knew that the rest of the organization knew it too ‚Äî we all knew it. We knew it from the moment that we said goodbye to Thibs (Tom Thibodeau) and we hired Mike (Brown), that we were gonna be in a hot spot because we just made the conferen

item.title: "To beat San Antonio, you have to have shooting," the ‚Ä¶
item.description: "To beat San Antonio, you have to have shooting," the executive said. "The Knicks have that." The East champs enter this series on an all-time hot streak on offense, shooting 41% from 3-point range during their 11-game win streak and outscoring their opponents by an NBA-record 262 points over that span.

item.title: "If it's a half-court game, it's advantage Spurs. I ‚Ä¶
item.description: "The Knicks have to keep playing fast," the West assistant coach said. "If it's a half-court game, it's advantage Spurs. I don't think Shai played fast enough in the Spurs series. They weren't doubling him, but he wasn't getting to the spots and lanes he needed. ... "If Victor isn't at the rim, the Knicks can finish over San Antonio's other guys."

item.title: ‚ÄúIt means a lot for my mother. When she emigrated from ‚Ä¶
item.description: Knicks‚Äô Karl-Anthony Towns on what it means to help the Knicks return to the Finals: ‚ÄúIt means a lot for my mother. When she emigrated from [the Dominican Republic] to New York, she saw Madison Square Garden for the first time and saw the energy the city has for Madison Square Garden and the Knicks. My mom, even to the day she passed, wasn‚Äôt big on the NBA rules, but one thing she did know was that only the best of the best perform and play at Madison Square Garden. To be able to have this momen

item.title: The way he's played over these first couple years of ‚Ä¶
item.description: It appears Castle has Brunson's utmost respect and attention before this series begins Wednesday. "He's great. I think his intensity and tenacity is special," Brunson said during media day at San Antonio's Frost Bank Center on Tuesday. "He plays with a chip on his shoulder. He's had that since I've seen him at UConn. The way he's played over these first couple years of his career, he's going to be a great player, great defender."

item.title: "Trying to be as disciplined as I can, crowd his ‚Ä¶
item.description: "I think what's worked for me is trying to be physical with him," Castle said. "He's obviously shorter than me, but he's a very physical guard, he gets to his spots well. Uses deception well, has great footwork. "Trying to be as disciplined as I can, crowd his space, but not give him the angles that he's looking for. At this point he's seen pretty much every coverage, been guarded all kinds of ways."

item.title: While this historic Knicks run in the postseason has ‚Ä¶
item.description: That includes the New York Knicks too, by the way. While the Eastern Conference champs are riding high now, it‚Äôs worth remembering that Antetokounmpo showed legitimate interest in playing for them last summer. And while this historic Knicks run in the postseason has certainly lessened the likelihood of that scenario materializing, you never know how the team‚Äôs stakeholders might feel if these finals don‚Äôt go their way.

item.title: One former Kings employee texted me that the moves ‚Ä¶
item.description: Sacramento lost in the Play-In Tournament in 2024. The Kings, however, still signed Brown to a contract extension. The commitment to Brown didn‚Äôt last, as he was fired two days after Christmas following a 13-18 start. Brown landed with the Knicks last May ‚Äî and look at him now. One former Kings employee texted me that the moves were ‚Äúf‚Äî‚Äìg stupid,‚Äù and that‚Äôs saying a lot, given the Kings‚Äô history of blunders in the draft, free agency and coaching hires over the last 20 years.

item.description: The hottest Instagram spot in Manhattan isn‚Äôt a new restaurant or bagel shop. It‚Äôs a subway entrance. Just a stone‚Äôs throw from Madison Square Garden, at 34th Street and Eighth Avenue, tucked away in the shadow of one of the most beloved spaces in the city, the entrance serving the A, C and E trains has become an unlikely attraction after being painted orange and blue in honor of the New York Knicks‚Äô first NBA Finals appearance in 27 years. Fans yelled ‚ÄúGo, Knicks!‚Äù as they walked by. ‚ÄúIt‚Äôs definitely become like a spectacle,‚Äù said Knicks fan Khairi Price, a 35-year-old photographer from Jamaica, Queens. Ribeiro had an idea. ‚ÄúHow about we paint the subway entrance Knicks colors?‚Äù Rieara recalled Tuesday afternoon while relaying the story in front of the now-famous subway entrance. ‚ÄúAnd we‚Äôre like, ‚ÄòOK. Let‚Äôs see if our colleagues in transit will allow us to do so.‚Äô‚Äù Those conversations began last Friday afternoon and continued through Saturday. By Sunday, R

That's always how I felt about analytics."

item.title: ‚ÄúI wear them every day. Feel naked without them,‚Äù ‚Ä¶
item.description: Walt ‚ÄúClyde‚Äù Frazier proudly held both fists out, displaying a ring on each hand. The one on his left was for being named one of the Top 50 NBA players of all-time in 1996. On the right hand, the New York Knicks legend wore a ring from the 1973 NBA championship, the last title the Knicks won. ‚ÄúI wear them every day. Feel naked without them,‚Äù Frazier told Andscape before the Knicks‚Äô Game 4 victory over the Cleveland Cavaliers to advance to the 2026 NBA Finals.

China. It‚Äôs an international sport now. But [the Knicks fan] hoopla is uncanny. And you see it when we go on the road. They just take over places. They are unequivocally the No. 1 fan. ‚Ä¶ ‚ÄúFor the [Knicks] fans, they deserve it for the respect they give. I‚Äôve been an ambassador for 50 years. That‚Äôs how long it‚Äôs been since our

item.title: ‚ÄúIn 1973, we were injured most of the season,‚Äù Frazier ‚Ä¶
item.description: The Knicks, who beat the Lakers 4-1 in the 1973 NBA Finals for their most recent championship, have the fifth-longest title drought in the NBA behind the Sacramento Kings (1951), Atlanta Hawks (1958), Phoenix Suns (founded in 1968, no titles) and Los Angeles Clippers (founded in 1970, no titles). Frazier said today‚Äôs Knicks remind him of the 1973 squad. ‚ÄúIn 1973, we were injured most of the season,‚Äù Frazier said. ‚ÄúBut coming towards the playoffs, we started to get healthy. It reminds me of what

Hart smiled, holding in any clap back he might have had cued up, and then acknowledged the moment with the emotion that it deserved. ‚ÄúIt‚Äôs something that is surreal,‚Äù Hart answered. ‚ÄúWhenever you‚Äôre in college and in that locker room, you know the goal is the NBA. You know the percent chance of you all being on the same team is slim, if not none. It‚Äôs something you talk

item.title: Bridges started his college career at Villanova as a ‚Ä¶
item.description: Seeing the relationship between these two up close, I became curious to learn more about the dynamic in college. That is when I learned that Hart hasn‚Äôt always played the role of protector for Bridges. In fact, it was the contrary. He used to try to punk him. Bridges started his college career at Villanova as a redshirt. Hart was an upperclassman and hated practicing (he still does). To get the most out of Hart, Wildcat coaches would try to trigger Hart with words. His head coach, Jay Wright, wo

item.title: ‚ÄúHe was a bully when I first got to campus,‚Äù Bridges ‚Ä¶
item.description: The relationship started off more adversarial. ‚ÄúHe was a bully when I first got to campus,‚Äù Bridges said. ‚ÄúI had nothing wrong with him. He didn‚Äôt like me at the beginning. There might have been a little fear of a 6-foot-6 lanky kid who was looking pretty solid. I think he had a little fear factor.‚Äù

item.title: ‚ÄúThe Josh Hart you guys see now, he was that from Day ‚Ä¶
item.description: Everyone around the Villanova program acknowledged how much Hart intentionally tried to terrorize Bridges back then. Yet, as time progressed, coaches started to view it as the best thing to happen to Bridges. Many believe it helped make him the player we see today, the one who can rise above adversity the same way his midrange jumper rises above defenders. ‚ÄúThe Josh Hart you guys see now, he was that from Day 1,‚Äù Villanova assistant coach Ashley Howard told The Athletic. ‚ÄúHe was fierce, tough, n

item.title: ‚ÄúAny time Josh got his shot blocked and there was a ‚Ä¶
item.description: Some viewed it as tough love, even if Hart never made his intentions known. ‚ÄúAny time Josh got his shot blocked and there was a change of possession, the next time Mikal got the ball, whether it was on the break or chasing him down or within the half court, it was like big brother versus little brother,‚Äù Villanova assistant coach Mike Nardi said. ‚ÄúJosh tried to send a message. We all believe that helped lead to Mikal becoming who he is. They were competing every single day.‚Äù

ESPN reported earlier Wednesday morning that Robinson was expected to be available for Game 1.

Monroe, a Hall of Famer and member of the Knicks team to win a championship in 1973, appeared at the Nassau County municipal building in Mineola, talking politics and reminiscing with County Executive Bruce Blakeman, who is also the GOP-nominee in this year‚Äôs governor‚Äôs race.

item.description: Mitchell Robinson‚Äôs status had become a central Knicks storyline after he had surgery for a fracture of the fifth metacarpal in his right hand between the conference finals and the Finals. There was no way I was gonna miss this.‚Äù

item.title: Multiple¬ÝKnicks fans were arrested Wednesday night ‚Ä¶
item.description: Multiple¬ÝKnicks fans were arrested Wednesday night while celebrating their hometown team‚Äôs thrilling NBA Finals Game 1 victory at Madison Square Garden‚Äôs¬Ýfirst outdoor watch party permitted by the city¬Ýin weeks. The celebratory sea of Knicks fanatics packed the streets surrounding the Garden, where they cheered the team‚Äôs 105-95 win against the San Antonio Spurs as hundreds of cops looked on ‚Äî and a waving Mayor Zohran Mamdani was driven by in a black SUV,¬Ýaccording to¬Ýsocial media clips.

item.title: ‚ÄúWe got to get him moving in space and toward the rim, ‚Ä¶
item.description: The Knicks scored 50 points in the paint. Wembanyama didn‚Äôt protect the area he normally owns. He didn‚Äôt make himself the daunting threat on both ends of the court that we‚Äôve become accustomed to watching. He scored nearly half of his points (12) from the free-throw line. ‚ÄúWe got to get him moving in space and toward the rim, whether that‚Äôs on rolls or running in transition,‚Äù Spurs coach Mitch Johnson said. ‚ÄúBut we need the pressure on the rim and the force in the paint.‚Äù

item.title: When three front office¬Ýexecutives¬Ýweighed in ‚Ä¶
item.description: This is the problem for the Spurs, though, in a matchup with the Knicks. As I¬Ýwrote last month, when three front office¬Ýexecutives¬Ýweighed in anonymously on the challenge of dealing with Wemby, the Knicks were noted as one of the few teams well-equipped to handle him. The rest of the league will likely feel compelled to add even more big, agile defenders in the months to come, but it comes in handy for New York that its roster is already set just right.

item.description: Knicks fans traveled well to San Antonio and the hometown faithful didn‚Äôt love it. item.title: A few factors will prevent the NBA from seeing truly ‚Ä¶
item.description: A few factors will prevent the NBA from seeing truly exceptional viewership numbers. For one, ABC will not separately count streaming viewership the way NBC does through Adobe Analytics. Game 3 of the Western Conference Finals, for example, averaged 8.3 million viewers when combining TV and streaming, but only 7.029 million on NBC alone. The combined streaming number surpasses the corresponding ECF game, but the TV number alone does not. Peacock is a much cheaper streaming service than ESPN Unli

item.title: The NBA will also face competition from the World Cup ‚Ä¶
item.description: The other problem for the NBA is the schedule. The Conference Finals were played every other day, which resulted in a consistent and predictable schedule. But that won‚Äôt be the case for the NBA Finals. There will be one-day breaks between Games 1 and 2, and 3 and 4, but two-day breaks between the rest of the games. The NBA will also face competition from the World Cup among casual audiences, though the schedule is set so that there will be no direct competition from the USMNT. Still, the inconsi

The promotion, called ‚Äú53 Years. 53 Real Ingredients,‚Äù links the Finals to a shared milestone from 1973, the Knicks‚Äô last championship season and the start of San Antonio‚Äôs professional basketball era.

item.title: Carmelo Anthony surprised a 12-year-old boy with ‚Ä¶

The gesture, captured and distributed on various social media platforms, shows Carmelo Rios opening a box from the athletic gear giant Fanatics and uncovering a New York Knicks jersey with his name on the back. Then, he pops open a computer to see a video message from Anthony, who tells him about the free tickets.
