item.description: The Knicks will host the Spurs at Madison Square Garden on June 8 and 10, and, if needed, will play here again on June 16. item.title: Officials say they'll be ready if there's an NBA ‚Ä¶
item.description: Officials say they'll be ready if there's an NBA Finals game in Manhattan on June 16, the same day of the World Cup match between France and Senegal at the New Jersey stadium. "This is a moment we've been preparing for, we've been hoping for and we feel fully prepared for," Mamdani said. At the press conference, state and local officials also detailed security arrangements designed to reduce the risk of crime and terrorism.

item.title: The ruckus started after Elmo shared an X post on ‚Ä¶
item.description: Elmo, the beloved red monster from "Sesame Street," sparked controversy this week with an innocuous social media post about the 2026 NBA Finals. The ruckus started after Elmo shared an X post on Wednesday -- as well as a matching post on Instagram -- wishing both the New York Knicks and the San Antonio Spurs well in their first Finals matchup at the Frost Bank Center in Texas. "Elmo hopes both teams have fun!" the post read.

item.title: Elmo's neutral stance drew sharp criticism from Knicks ‚Ä¶

(A stretch of Manhattan's West 63rd Street was notably renamed Sesame Street in 2019 in honor of the children's program's 50th anniversary, and Elmo himself attended the unveiling ceremony.) "This isn't

item.title: Mitchell Robinson upgraded to available for Game 2
item.description: The Knicks have upgraded Mitchell Robinson (right hand fractured 5th metacarpal) to available for Friday‚Äôs Game 2 against San Antonio.

item.title: As President Donald Trump prepares to watch his ‚Ä¶
item.description: As President Donald Trump prepares to watch his hometown New York Knicks take on the San Antonio Spurs at Madison Square Garden next week, local and federal officials are planning for a heightened security posture, according to law enforcement sources familiar with the planning.

item.title: A hard closure is expected around the entirety of ‚Ä¶
item.description: A hard closure is expected around the entirety of Madison Square Garden. Officials are planning for no watch parties, no vehicle access and no pedestrian travel through the secured area, which sits in a busy part of Midtown Manhattan and is above a major transit hub. The Seventh and Eighth Avenue corridors around the arena are expected to be closed.

item.title: No bags will be permitted, and attendees are advised ‚Ä¶
item.description: Only ticketed fans will be allowed to enter Madison Square Garden, and they will go through Secret Service-level screening before entering ‚Äî similar to the Transportation Security Administration's screening process at airports. No bags will be permitted, and attendees are advised not to bring purses, backpacks or totes of any size, law enforcement sources said.

item.title: Outside of the four-straight Cavaliers-Warriors NBA ‚Ä¶
item.description: Sports Media Watch: Outside of the four-straight Cavaliers-Warriors NBA Finals, Knicks-Spurs was the most-watched Game 1 of the Finals in 25 years (Sixers-Lakers 2001):

item.title: NYPD Commissioner Jessica Tisch said police would be ‚Ä¶
item.description: NYPD Commissioner Jessica Tisch said police would be out in force Friday evening for Game 2 of the Knicks NBA Finals run, cautioning fans to be on their best behavior across the city‚Äôs three official watch parties. She said that the NYPD will have about 1,000 officers out on Friday night at the two Madison Square Garden Events‚Äîone ticketed indoor watch party and one outdoor event in Plaza 33 just outside the Garden.

item.title: ‚Äú‚ÄäIf you see a Spurs fan, do not engage, back away,‚Äù ‚Ä¶
item.description: Tisch cautioned fans who might be tempted to overcelebrate. She said that chaos and destruction were ‚Äúnot the flex you think it is‚Äù and offered advice to die-hard Knicks fans. ‚Äú‚ÄäIf you see a Spurs fan, do not engage, back away,‚Äù Tisch said. ‚ÄúBelieve me, after Game 1, they have been through enough pain and suffering.‚Äù

item.title: Jeremy Lin to attend Games 3 and 4 in Madison Square Garden return
item.description: Stefan Bondy: Jeremy Lin tells @Erich Richter that he plans to return to MSG for the first time as a fan for Games 3 & 4

item.title: Knicks shock San Antonio again, take 2-0 lead in the NBA Finals
item.description: The New York Knicks are two games away from their first NBA championship since 1973. The Knicks held on for a thrilling 105-104 Game 2 win against the San Antonio Spurs in the 2026 NBA Finals on Friday night. They lead the series 2-0 thanks to two road wins and will now head to Madison Square Garden with a chance to potentially clinch a title.

item.title: Best point differential in NBA playoff history: ‚Ä¶
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Best point differential in NBA playoff history: 2013-14 Spurs: +214, 2016-17 Warriors: +230, 2025-26 KNICKS: +282. A run unlike any other.

item.title: The Knicks are the second team in NBA history to win ‚Ä¶
item.description: NBA Communications: The Knicks are the second team in NBA history to win 13 straight games in a single postseason, joining the 2016-17 Warriors (15 straight). With their eighth road straight win, the Knicks have tied the 2000-01 Lakers for the longest road winning streak in a single postseason.

item.title: The Knicks are the third team in NBA Finals history to ‚Ä¶
item.description: NBA Communications: The Knicks are the third team in NBA Finals history to win the first two games of a series on the road: 1993 ‚Äî Bulls (vs. Suns); 1995 ‚Äî Rockets (vs. Magic); 2026 ‚Äî Knicks (vs. Spurs)

item.title: Victor Wembanyama's final 30 seconds:
item.description: Law Murray: Victor Wembanyama's final 30 seconds: - missed 2-for-1 shot - live ball turnover with game tied - foul on Jalen Brunson (go-ahead NY FT) - missed game-winning FG

item.title: Victor Wembanyama: 'I need to have more poise, more control over the game'
item.description: ClutchPoints: "I'm still very blurry, and that's the whole problem. I need to have more poise, more control over the game. That's the general image" Victor Wembanyama was unable to recall the final 3 possessions of the game üò≥

item.description: Fullcourtpass: Wemby on his matchup with Karl-Anthony Towns "It's very different from previous series. It's bringing us into difficult areas because they're good players, he's a good player. We need to figure it out.‚Äù

item.title: Wembanyama on the pass to Steph Castle‚Äôs back in the ‚Ä¶
item.description: Jared Weiss: Victor Wembanyama on the pass to Steph Castle‚Äôs back in the final seconds of the Knicks‚Äô NBA Finals Game 2 win: ‚ÄúI threw that one away. I messed up. We needed to win that game. This game was ours. But at this point, it‚Äôs done. Am I going to regret it? Yes, of course. Am I going to use that to fuel me and to fuel us next game? Absolutely.‚Äù

item.title: ‚ÄúI was looking at him when he first got the rebound ‚Ä¶
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Steph Castle on Wemby‚Äôs pass that hit his back: ‚ÄúI was looking at him when he first got the rebound and I just started to take off, trying to give him some space to dribble up the court. I didn't see him throw it to me.‚Äù

item.description: SNY Knicks: "I saw he wasn't looking so I just tried to go get it‚Äù. Jalen Brunson talks about his steal in the final seconds leading to his free throws:

Robinson: ‚ÄúBeen here for 8 years, now here we are in the Finals. I can truly say I done seen it all.‚Äù

item.description: SNY Knicks: ‚ÄúMy door is always open. I don't care who's idea it is that brings me an idea, I want to know. It's my decision at the end of the day to say yes or no for the group." Mike Brown talks about his leadership and coaching philosophy:

item.title: Knicks this postseason: üî• 43 days since their last ‚Ä¶
item.description: ESPN Insights: The New York Knicks this postseason: üî• 43 days since their last loss üî• Third team to win Game 1 & 2 of NBA Finals on the road üî• Heading into Game 3 on the 2nd-longest single-season win streak in NBA playoff history üî• 8th straight road playoff win

item.title: Mitchell Robinson expected to draw significant free agent¬Ýinterest¬Ýthis summer
item.description: A similar trajectory appears possible this summer for the Knicks' Mitchell Robinson. Hartenstein's heir to the backup center spot in Gotham is expected to draw significant free agent¬Ýinterest¬Ýthis summer, league¬Ýsources¬Ýsay. Robinson only logged 14 minutes in New York's Game 2 victory, but he drew considerable postgame praise from Knicks coach Mike Brown and teammate Karl-Anthony Towns for his one-on-one defense against Wembanyama.

item.title: Joe Clements happened to be sitting near Sandra ‚Ä¶
item.description: A man went viral after thanking Jalen Brunson‚Äôs mom for creating the Knicks star. Joe Clements happened to be sitting near Sandra Brunson in the stands in San Antonio after Game 1 of the NBA Finals ‚Äî so he used the moment to express his gratitude. ‚ÄúHey, Mrs. Brunson, Thank you so much for having that boy. He is the franchise changer. We love him,‚Äù¬ÝClements said in the video,¬Ýwhich racked up 1.7 million views, and hundreds of comments from fans, calling her the ‚ÄúQueen of NYC.‚Äù

item.title: Wembanyama came out last again and again acknowledged ‚Ä¶
item.description: Thanks to another invite from the Kahlig Auto Group, four sisters attended Wednesday's Game 1 loss to the Knicks. Before the game, the sisters once again gathered in the tunnel. Kornet, after passing them by, came back and thanked them for being there. Wembanyama came out last again and again acknowledged them. Soon after, they were standing courtside when a familiar voice asked them to turn around. "Sisters, could you please pray for me?"¬ÝWembanyama asked them.

item.title: ‚ÄúMy desire is still the same. I‚Äôd like to have a ‚Ä¶
item.description: Jeremy Lin and Carmelo Anthony appear to be taking steps toward resolving lingering questions about their relationship from their Knicks days. Lin revealed that both sides have been in contact regarding a possible appearance on Anthony‚Äôs podcast, though he would prefer to speak privately first. ‚ÄúMy desire is still the same. I‚Äôd like to have a private conversation before having a public recorded conversation,‚Äù Lin said.

item.title: ‚ÄúI can guess [what the issues are], but I don‚Äôt think ‚Ä¶
item.description: Anthony recently invited Lin onto his show, saying he wants to ‚Äúclear the air‚Äù and address stories that have followed them for years. Lin said he isn‚Äôt certain what issues Anthony wants to discuss and doesn‚Äôt want to speculate. ‚ÄúI can guess [what the issues are], but I don‚Äôt think it would do Melo justice for me to guess what I think he might mean.‚Äù

item.title: A viewing party outside Madison Square Garden, where ‚Ä¶
item.description: A viewing party outside Madison Square Garden, where several thousand Knicks fans had gathered to watch Game 2 of the N.B.A. finals, erupted into chaos on Friday night after the Knicks won. Revelers scaled light poles and food carts, blocked traffic and, in one case, punched a police officer in the face, according to the authorities.

item.title: The New York Police Department said it took a total of ‚Ä¶
item.description: The New York Police Department said it took a total of 26 people into custody as the party escalated, arresting 17 people and releasing another nine with court summonses for disorderly conduct. Several people resisted arrest or otherwise refused to comply with police orders, the department said. And one person, who has not been publicly identified, punched a 23-year-old female police officer in the face after jumping a barrier into a blocked-off area. The same person later bit a different office

item.title: Knicks' interest in Giannis Antetokounmpo could increase if they lose in Finals?
item.description: Heat Central: Chris Haynes on Giannis: ‚ÄúI think this is the closest he‚Äôs come to saying he wants to be moved without saying he wants to be moved. I think signs are pointing to that‚Ä¶. This game (finals) is going to have some implications as well. If the Knicks were to falter to the Spurs, they might be in play for Giannis.‚Äù

item.title: On Luke Kornet kick ball late, NBA says: ‚ÄúAlthough ‚Ä¶
item.description: Ian Begley: In its 2-minute report, NBA finds that referees in Game 2 of Knicks-Spurs made no errors in final 2 minutes. Every call was either a correct call or a correct non-call. On Luke Kornet kick ball late, NBA says: ‚ÄúAlthough Kornet (SAS) lifts his foot, he does not intentionally kick the loose ball.‚Äù

item.title: The assault on an NYPD officer outside Madison Square ‚Ä¶
item.description: Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani: The assault on an NYPD officer outside Madison Square Garden last night was unacceptable. New Yorkers are rightfully excited about the Knicks' historic Finals run, and we want fans to celebrate this moment together. There is, however, no place for violence, and no tolerance for attacks on police officers. Thank you to the officers who worked to keep fans safe throughout the night, and we wish the injured officer a speedy recovery. The overwhelming majority of New York

item.description: NBA Base: The Jalen Brunson camp reportedly "laughed" at the Mavericks when Dallas tried to offer him a 4-year, $55M extension near the 2022 trade deadline, per @TheSteinLine "The Brunson camp basically laughed at them and said, 'Uh, we'll take our chances in free agency now, you guys had your chance.' ... The Mavs still had the option at that point of picking up the phone and saying, 'Jalen, we completely screwed this up, we are deeply sorry, we've disrespected you, we want to make this right,

item.title: Anthony Edwards unhappy with Timberwolves' roster?
item.description: Brian Windhorst: Going forward after this series, the Minnesota Timberwolves future is in question. Somewhat because of what Anthony Edwards feels about the roster, and I wonder if KAT was still there, if he did.

item.description: New York Knicks: Knicks announce no-bag policy for Game 3, say fans should get to the game two hours early to give time for 'screening'.

item.title: NBA rescinds technical foul on Mitchell Robinson for dustup with Victor Wembanyama
item.description: Chris Haynes: NBA has rescinded the technical foul Mitchell Robinson received after a back and forth shoving match with Victor Wembanyama last night. Wemby did not receive a tech.

item.title: Mitchell Robinson's (NYK) technical foul against ‚Ä¶
item.description: NBA Official: Mitchell Robinson's (NYK) technical foul against Victor Wembanyama (SAS) at 4:56 of the 2nd quarter in Game 2 of the NBA Finals on 6/5/26 has been rescinded upon league review.

item.title: For a few hours, one of America‚Äôs most notorious jail ‚Ä¶
item.description: For a few hours, one of America‚Äôs most notorious jail complexes and least visible institutions is tapping into a civic ritual that has brought New Yorkers together like few public events in decades. The underdog Knicks‚Äô long-awaited return to basketball‚Äôs biggest stage has given people in custody at Rikers a rare opportunity to participate in a shared New York moment that will see them arguing over the same calls, second-guessing the same decisions and dreaming the same dream that is consuming t

item.title: ‚ÄúIt‚Äôs the chemistry and the teamwork that makes them ‚Ä¶
item.description: ‚ÄúYou don‚Äôt have a team full of superstar players in the Knicks,‚Äù says Luis Guzman, a 43-year-old from the Pelham Parkway section of the Bronx who has been held at Rikers since September on a burglary case that remains pending. ‚ÄúIt‚Äôs the chemistry and the teamwork that makes them great. This is the year they finally might get it done. If we take one in San Antonio, it‚Äôs over for San Antonio. We will not lose at home. All we got to do is take one down here.‚Äù

item.title: He is part of what correction officials describe as an ‚Ä¶
item.description: Guzman has taken a front-row seat for Wednesday‚Äôs game inside the Beacon Center, a common area that includes classrooms, a recording studio, a barbershop and other workforce-training programs. He is part of what correction officials describe as an ‚Äúhonors house‚Äù, a housing unit reserved for incarcerated people who have gone at least 120 days without violence or disciplinary incidents and have demonstrated a sustained commitment to programming and rehabilitation, qualifying them to receive snacks

item.title: Some of the items are similar to what people in ‚Ä¶
item.description: The reward for good behavior on Wednesday night looks more like a Super Bowl spread than standard jailhouse fare: bags of chips and cookies, platters of fruit and cheese, bottled soft drinks and a large sheet cake frosted with three words that, for a night at least, unite nearly everyone in attendance: Let‚Äôs Go Knicks! Some of the items are similar to what people in custody could buy through commissary, though one DOC staffer notes that the name brands represent an upgrade from what‚Äôs offered du

item.title: The Game 1 watch parties on Rikers extend far beyond ‚Ä¶
item.description: The Game 1 watch parties on Rikers extend far beyond this one. Correction officials say incarcerated people in roughly 44 housing units across the complex are taking part in some form of gathering for the game, reaching almost 2,000 people in all. Some facilities host viewings in dedicated program spaces like the Peace Center in the basement of the Robert N Davoren Complex, a similar hub for incarcerated males aged 21 and under. Over in the Otis Bantum Correctional Center, another male detention

item.title: ‚ÄúThey keep putting it off, putting it off,‚Äù says ‚Ä¶
item.description: Thomas Gregory, 59, arrived at Rikers on a charge of attempted criminal possession of a weapon nearly 15 months ago and is still waiting for his case to move forward. A longtime Knicks supporter going back the Walt Frazier days, he says he was supposed to go to trial in December but has instead watched hearing after hearing come and go. With another court date looming next week, he speaks about his future with a mix of frustration and resignation. ‚ÄúThey keep putting it off, putting it off,‚Äù says

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns eligible for $272 million extension
item.description: If the Knicks do, in fact, tie the bow on this NBA Finals package, there will still be a discussion to be had with Karl-Anthony Towns about securing his services for the long-term. He has one guaranteed season left on his current deal, with a player option worth $61 million for the 2027-28 campaign, and is eligible for a massive extension (four years and a combined $272 million).

item.title: Along the way, with Karl-Anthony Towns leading the ‚Ä¶
item.description: Along the way, with Karl-Anthony Towns leading the Knicks in playoff plus-minus while averaging 17.3 points, 10.8 rebounds and 5.6 assists in the playoffs, it has become quite evident that he deserves the kind of respect that some of his peers have been loath to give over the years (from Jimmy Butler on down the line). His defense against Spurs big man Victor Wembanyama in this series‚Äô first two games should be enough to reshape how he‚Äôs viewed. That this revelatory showing comes after Randle st

item.title: What‚Äôs scary is that there is another level the Knicks ‚Ä¶
item.description: What‚Äôs scary is that there is another level the Knicks can reach.¬Ý Jalen Brunson in particular.¬Ý He has been far from his best across the first two games of the Finals ‚Äî he has shot just 19-for-56 from the field (33.9 percent) and 4-for-17 from 3-point range (23.5 percent). He‚Äôs committed a combined eight turnovers.¬Ý In¬ÝFriday‚Äôs 105-104 Game 2 win¬Ýover the Spurs at Frost Bank Center,¬Ýhe missed three straight shots¬Ýas the Spurs went on a 14-0 run to tie the game late in the fourth quarter.

item.title: ‚ÄúDon‚Äôt be afraid to fail.‚Äù I‚Äôve always been afraid to ‚Ä¶
item.description: Watching Jalen Brunson relentlessly try to will New York back into games in fourth quarters, not caring if one shot doesn‚Äôt go in because he trusts his work has been a reminder to me to believe in myself and my track record. I‚Äôve been doing this a long time with a lot to show for it. Watching Mike Brown tinker with lineups, strategies and philosophies from one game to the next has made think, ‚ÄúDon‚Äôt be afraid to fail.‚Äù I‚Äôve always been afraid to fail. ‚ÄúIt‚Äôs all about being able to say you gave i

item.title: Guard¬ÝJose Alvarado, a trade deadline acquisition, has ‚Ä¶
item.description: But under coach Mike Brown this season, New York has leaned on its bench more than it ever did with Thibodeau. That's especially true in this playoff run, which is headed back to MSG for Monday's Game 3 of the Finals against the¬ÝSan Antonio Spurs¬Ý(8:30 p.m. ET, ABC). Up and down the Knicks bench is evidence of a pie being divided more evenly: Beyond Shamet's contributions, guard¬ÝMiles McBride¬Ýhas averaged career highs in minutes, scoring and 3-point percentage. Big man¬ÝMitchell Robinson¬Ýhas stay

item.title: ‚ÄúIt was huge,‚Äù Brown said. ‚ÄúPop ‚Äî I talk about MSG ‚Ä¶
item.description: Brown has coached some of the league‚Äôs biggest stars, including LeBron James and Kobe Bryant. He coached under some of the most venerated coaches, including Popovich and Steve Kerr. But now, for the first time in his career, the Larry O‚ÄôBrien Trophy is within his grasp as the head of a locker room. Brown points to his time under Popovich as one of the biggest reasons he has arrived at this moment. ‚ÄúIt was huge,‚Äù Brown said. ‚ÄúPop ‚Äî I talk about MSG being iconic, New York City being iconic ‚Äî Pop i

item.title: ‚ÄúSprays‚Äù ‚Äî his word for plays on which a player inside ‚Ä¶
item.description: Those are notable, but they do not take the crown. The biggest upset, by far, is that it took Knicks coach Mike Brown 1,276 words into his ebullient, borderline-ecstatic stream-of-consciousness news conference after his team‚Äôs¬ÝGame 2 win¬Ýbefore he finally blurted out the word ‚Äúspray.‚Äù ‚ÄúSprays‚Äù ‚Äî his word for plays on which a player inside the 3-point line kicks the ball out to a player beyond the arc, whether as a result of a drive, catch, offensive board or divine intervention ‚Äî might be the de

item.description: This year, only one four-man unit crossed the¬Ý900-minute¬Ýmark. (Lineups featuring Robinson, Anunoby, Bridges and Brunson were a plus-19.6 points per 100 possessions, thanks in large part to Robinson's offensive rebounds and "sprays," Brown's term for getting in the paint and kicking out to shooters. I'm sorry about the way the circumstances are contractually. I have nothing to do [with] that. I believe you

item.title: In contrast, I will now fanboy my socks off because ‚Ä¶
item.description: You are, of course, allowed to roll your eyes at the prospect of reading yet another profile of a celebrity NBA fan. And especially a celebrity New York Knicks fan. The four-time Emmy Award-winning actress ‚Äî the fierce co-star of the seminal HBO series ‚ÄúThe Sopranos‚Äù with the late, great James Gandolfini, and who went on to more acclaim as the star of the Showtime series ‚ÄúNurse Jackie‚Äù ‚Äî is, like many fellow

item.title: ‚ÄúI‚Äôm a little embarrassed at my positivity and joie de ‚Ä¶
item.description: ‚ÄúI‚Äôm a little embarrassed at my positivity and joie de vivre,‚Äù she says. ‚ÄúNot being the winning team is kind of built into the Knicks‚Äô sauce. You say you‚Äôre a Knicks fan, and people are like, ‚ÄòOh, I‚Äôm sorry.‚Äô It‚Äôs like a joke. But it‚Äôs never really mattered to me ‚Äôcause I just love ‚Äôem. I think I wouldn't make him wait all the way to the 2nd round"

item.title: New York City mayor Zohran Mamdani is keenly aware of ‚Ä¶

item.description: Mamdani and the NYPD have worked with the Knicks to keep the events going, though they needed late sign-offs for Games 1 and 2. The events have not been without incident. The NYPD arrested 17 people during Game 2, and one officer was injured. ‚ÄúThis is the constant bounce in any time of immense excitement and joy is that we want to make sure that it is safe, it is secure, and also that we are not stifling a moment that New Yorkers are going to remember for a long time,‚Äù Mamdani said. ‚ÄúAnd I think

And to me, the team that appreciates the position we're in the most will be the o

item.title: Josh Hart on why Jalen Brunson could be the king of New York: Because he doesn't care about it
item.description: His college roommate and longtime teammate Josh Hart has a theory as to why, after so many have tried and failed to be the savior of the Knicks -- and de facto king of New York -- it just might end up being Brunson. "Because I don't think he came in that way. He doesn't care about it," Hart said. "He just wants to win. When you have that humility, all the other stuff takes care of itself."

"All I know is he had the same things that Hardaway and Nash had -- heart, brains and balls," Nelson told ESPN. "Those are things that generally don't fit into an analytics model."

item.title: "I think Jalen had a loyalty to the Mavs because ‚Ä¶
item.description: By that point, Rose had represented both Brunsons as an agent. When Leon left for the Knicks, son Sam Rose joined Jalen Brunson's representation team. In 2022, less than a month before Jalen Brunson was set to hit free agency, Leon Rose hired Rick Brunson as an assistant coach on Tom Thibodeau's staff. "I think Jalen had a loyalty to the Mavs because they'd drafted him, but the Knicks were his actual family," said a Mavericks source. "I don't think we fully grasped that."

item.title: Jalen Brunson tonight: 3/9 in the paint
item.description: ‚Ä™Bobby Krivitsky‚Ä¨: Jalen Brunson tonight: 3/9 in the paint He was great when he got all the way to the rim, but the Spurs did an excellent job of making life difficult for him when he tried to attack. No longer having Wemby guard KAT was a difference-maker.

item.title: The team that has won the turnover battle has won all ‚Ä¶
item.description: Law Murray: The team that has won the turnover battle has won all three games to begin the 2026 NBA Finals

item.title: Jose Alvarado went head-first into the first row and ‚Ä¶
item.description: Knick guard Jose Alvarado went head-first into the first row and former New York City Mayor Mike Bloom berg trying to retrieve a loose ball during Tuesday night‚Äôs Game 3 of the NBA Finals. The 84-year-old looked a little shaken up afterwards, but appeared otherwise unscathed.

item.title: "These guys are tired. It's just what comes with it. I ‚Ä¶
item.description: Myles Turner: "These guys are tired. It's just what comes with it. I don't understand what people think when I say tired. It's not just sleepy tired. It's not just like I'm a little fatigued. Mentally, emotionally, you're exhausted, bro. Like, side-to-side movement. People don't realize just, like, basketball is not just a one-dimensional sport where you're sprinting up and down the floor. Like, sliding laterally and what not, playing on different linear planes, that takes a lot out of you."

item.title: Spurs take Game 3 at MSG
item.description: James L. Edwards III‚Ä¨: New York's 13-game playoff win streak ends with a Game 3 loss. Knicks fans will have to wait at least 48 hours to see their team win a Finals game at MSG. New York now has to travel to San Antonio at least one more time. 13 turnovers for 21 San Antonio points.

item.title: The New York Knicks have lost a basketball game for ‚Ä¶
item.description: ‚Ä™Keerthika Uthayakumar‚Ä¨: The New York Knicks have lost a basketball game for the first time since April 23rd, 46 days ago. Their 13-game win streak is over.

item.title: I‚Äôve gotten used to it. I‚Äôve only been traded one ‚Ä¶
item.description: The NBA season could end this week in a way that it hasn‚Äôt in 53 years ‚Äì with the New York Knicks as league champions. The Knicks won the first two games in the best-of-seven title series against the San Antonio Spurs, with Game 3 on Monday night. Former Auburn standout Isaac Okoro‚Äôs NBA season ended two months ago, and he expects this offseason to be much like the last few: He‚Äôll be tuning out speculation about where he‚Äôll be traded. ‚ÄúI think I‚Äôll just control what I can control,‚Äù said Okoro, w

item.title: I've never seen a Kyrie-led team win a title.
item.description: Paul Pierce: "This sparked up some conversation with me and some of my friends. I'm like, "Hey, is Jalen Brunson better than Kyrie all time if he wins?" Man, hold on now. I have to agree with that because I've never seen a Kyrie-led team win a title. And as great as Kyrie has been in his career, there's gonna be some tough conversations made if or when the Knicks win this title about Jalen Brunson and where he stands in NBA history as one of the better guards."

item.title: Knicks lost both the possession battle (net TSA) and ‚Ä¶
item.description: Automatic: Knicks lost both the possession battle (net TSA) and shooting battle (net TS%) tonight for the first time since Mar. 29th, 2026 against OKC.

item.title: Zohran Mamdani paid $1,000 for a standing-room-only ticket near the rafters
item.description: Trump, invited to attend Game 3 of the finals by Knicks owner James Dolan, was seated in a suite near midcourt, halfway up the stands, in a box surrounded by bulletproof glass constructed for his visit. NBA commissioner Adam Silver joined the suite in the second quarter and spoke with Trump, who was snacking on a bucket of fries and sipping from a Diet Coke bottle. Trump‚Äôs granddaughter Kai Trump was also spotted in the suite, as were Dolan, Environmental Protection Agency Administrator Lee Zeld

item.title: Reporters traveling with Trump counted two middle ‚Ä¶
item.description: Trump arrived in Manhattan just before 7:20 p.m. ET on Marine One, taking a short flight from New Jersey. The motorcade took the Franklin D. Roosevelt East River Drive from Wall Street up to the Garden, passing several Knicks watch parties along the way. Reporters traveling with Trump counted two middle fingers and one thumbs down, signs that said ‚ÄúNobody wants you here,‚Äù ‚ÄúTrump must go,‚Äù and ‚ÄúImpeach. Convict. Remove.‚Äù

item.title: Donald Trump wants to make tonight about himself, like ‚Ä¶
item.description: On the U.S. Senate floor in Washington on Monday, Charles Schumer, Democrat from New York, said, ‚ÄúTonight ought to be all about the game, the players and the fans. But Donald Trump wants to make tonight about himself, like he always does. ‚ÄúMidtown has to shut down. Bars near the Garden are about to take a huge hit on what should be their biggest night of the year. And free watch parties near the Garden have been canceled because Trump can‚Äôt bear letting anyone else be the center of attention for

item.title: NYPD had to contain rowdy Knicks fans at a watch ‚Ä¶
item.description: Legion Hoops: Warning. Graphic. NYPD had to contain rowdy Knicks fans at a watch party. This is horrible.

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns: 'We didn't do what got us 13 straight wins in a row'
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: KAT: "That ain't cost us the game. We turned the ball over, didn't execute, didn't do what got us 13 straight wins in a row. That's how you lose a game"

item.title: "Just don't bring in the motherf***ing ICE. No ICE. ‚Ä¶
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Spike Lee has one rule for a potential Knicks' championship parade: "Just don't bring in the motherf***ing ICE. No ICE. I've said this more than once, ICE is not welcome." (Via @andscape, h/t @TheNBABase)

item.title: "The officials are going to do their thing. There's ‚Ä¶
item.description: SNY Knicks: "The officials are going to do their thing. There's questionable calls every night, that's not something new. We can't sit here and make excuses" Landry Shamet responds to the officiating tonight:

item.title: Jalen Brunson on Wemby shoving him: "Whatever you saw ‚Ä¶
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Jalen Brunson on Wemby shoving him: "Whatever you saw is what you saw"

item.title: Jalen Brunson debuted a new Nike Kobe 6 PE colorway ‚Ä¶
item.description: colendri.com: Jalen Brunson debuted a new Nike Kobe 6 PE colorway against the San Antonio Spurs, finishing with 32 points, 5 rebounds, 5 assists in 35:27.

item.title: Rowdy Knicks fanatics clashed with cops outside Bryant ‚Ä¶
item.description: Rowdy Knicks fanatics clashed with cops outside Bryant Park‚Äôs Game 3 watch party Monday night ‚Äì including at least one who hurled a bottle at an officer. The sea of fans was already teetering over the edge just one hour after tip-off, as many didn‚Äôt make it inside the official watch park of the Knicks-Spurs game in the Midtown park. Huge brawls broke out and cops in riot gear rushed to control the unruly crowd, deploying pepper spray.

item.title: The troublemakers, who largely appeared to be young ‚Ä¶
item.description: The troublemakers, who largely appeared to be young people, even knocked down a metal bus sign and broke off large tree branches. Unruly youths took it in turns to try and knock over a bus sign near Bryant Park, wild video obtained by Freedom News shows. The circular sign eventually fell off after the pole had been rigorously shaken ‚Äì and the moment sparked jubilant scenes.

item.title: ‚ÄúHe's a nice guy, but you need a certain aptitude to ‚Ä¶
item.description: ‚ÄúThis president has no business showing up in New York City,‚Äù Smith said, adding that the move was ‚Äúselfish‚Äù and ‚Äúnarcissistic.‚Äù The longtime broadcaster even joked that he would blame Trump if the Knicks failed to deliver a victory. Trump's response arrived when reporters asked about Smith's comments. Rather than directly addressing the criticism, Trump questioned Smith's qualifications for political leadership. ‚ÄúHe's a nice guy, but you need a certain aptitude to run for president,‚Äù Trump said

item.title: Trump gets booed and heckled out of New York City as ‚Ä¶
item.description: ‚Ä™MeidasTouch‚Ä¨: Trump gets booed and heckled out of New York City as he departs Game 3 of the NBA Finals.

item.title: LeBron James says Josh Hart is like a wild card:
item.description: NBA Courtside: LeBron James says Josh Hart is like a wild card: ‚ÄúHe‚Äôs one of those few guys in our league that makes an impact and it doesn‚Äôt have to always be scoring. It‚Äôs the Draymond Green role where you know he gets you 8 points but it‚Äôs 12 rebounds. It‚Äôs 7 assists. It‚Äôs diving for loose ball‚Äôs, some the stuff that don‚Äôt show up in the box scores, you know. It‚Äôs that competitive will, you know, and if you disrespect him he can obviously make shots as well.‚Äù

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns has not yet managed to score a ‚Ä¶
item.description: ‚Ä™NBA Stat‚Ä¨: Crazy stat: Karl-Anthony Towns has not yet managed to score a single point in the three fourth quarters he's already played in the Finals. 1st / 2nd /3rd quarters: 50 points 4th quarter: 0 point #NBA

item.title: This is just the 2nd NBA Finals in which the home team ‚Ä¶
item.description: ‚Ä™NBA Stat‚Ä¨: This is just the 2nd NBA Finals in which the home team has lost each of the first 3 games of the series. The 1st such occurrence came in 1993, when the Chicago Bulls opened the Finals with two road wins over the Phoenix Suns before the Suns returned the favor in Game 3. (via @statitudes.com ) #NBA

item.title: Ben Stiller filming documentary at Knicks games
item.description: Knicks superfan Ben Stiller has been getting attention for the cool videos he‚Äôs been shooting on his iPhone from celebrity row and behind-the-scenes at the team‚Äôs games. But sources tell Page Six Hollywood there‚Äôs more to those mini-shoots than collecting footage for social media posts. ‚ÄúBen is filming a documentary for HBO,‚Äù said a showbiz source last night during the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden. ‚ÄúHe‚Äôs filming it on his phone.‚Äù (We‚Äôve reached out reps for Stiller and HBO.)

item.title: Many moguls sat in their own season seats. Jealous ‚Ä¶
item.description: But in Hollywood, any execs watching the game on TV in LA were, of course, more interested in which of their bosses, colleagues and competitors were at the game instead of them, and how much the tickets might‚Äôve cost. Many moguls sat in their own season seats. Jealous sources told us that WME and TKO exec Mark Shapiro was spotted sitting on the floor with former White House Chief of Staff Rahm Emanuel in WME‚Äôs seats. Next to them were Warner Bros. Discovery seats occupied by CEO David Zaslav, ‚ÄúC

item.title: Also seen at the home game the Knicks lost were ‚Ä¶
item.description: Also seen at the home game the Knicks lost were billionaire Jets owner Woody Johnson, ‚ÄúMarty Supreme‚Äù director Josh Safdie, Woody Allen, President Trump, Fat Joe, Mayor Mamdani, Derek Jeter, Alex Rodriguez Eli Manning, Rick Pitino, Francisco Lindor, Patrick Ewing, Walt ‚ÄúClyde‚Äù Frazier, Allan Houston, John Franco, Juan Soto, Todd Zeile and hockey stars Jonathan Quick, Adam Fox and Vincent Trocheck.

item.title: Pat McAfee pricelessly referred to former New York ‚Ä¶
item.description: ESPN anchor Pat McAfee pricelessly referred to former New York Mayor Michael Bloomberg as ‚Äúsome very rich guy‚Äù after the billionaire was nearly crushed in the crowd when Knicks guard Jose Alvarado leapt into the stands chasing a loose ball. ‚ÄúAlvarado‚Äôs sitting on some very rich guy,‚Äù McAfee joked on the alternate broadcast of Game 3 of the NBA Finals. ‚ÄúOh, that‚Äôs Mayor Bloomberg!‚Äù he quickly added after realizing who the courtside fan was.

item.title: The ‚ÄúDo the Right Thing‚Äù director sported a custom ‚Ä¶
item.description: Spike Lee is maybe hoping to give the Knicks an extra blessing on Monday night. The ‚ÄúDo the Right Thing‚Äù director sported a custom Pope Leo XIV Knicks jersey for Game 3 of the NBA Finals after receiving it at the Vatican last year. Pope Leo is a Villanova alum, like Knicks stars Jalen Brunson, Mikal Bridges and Josh Hart.

item.title: NBA to review Victor Wembanyama's first-quarter foul on Jalen Brunson
item.description: Marc Stein: The NBA, as is customary, will today review Victor Wembanyama‚Äôs first-quarter foul on Jalen Brunson to determine whether any additional penalties (such as a retroactive flagrant foul) are assessed.

item.title: President Donald Trump is not expected to attend Game ‚Ä¶
item.description: Shams Charania: President Donald Trump is not expected to attend Game 4 of Knicks-Spurs in the NBA Finals on Wednesday night at Madison Square Garden due to scheduling conflicts and obligations, sources tell ESPN. Trump, a lifelong Knicks fan, watched the majority of Game 3 from a suite.

item.title: Though his teammate AJ Dybantsa has garnered more of ‚Ä¶
item.description: Though his teammate AJ Dybantsa has garnered more of the headlines, as the projected No. 1 pick in the 2026 NBA draft, Keba Keita has consistently impressed NBA teams throughout the predraft process. ‚ÄúKeba is on the rise,‚Äù his agent, Keith Kreiter, said. ‚ÄúHe‚Äôs been outplaying bigs who are projected to go in the first round. His physicality and ability to guard one through five makes him a no-brainer for the mid-to-late second round.‚Äù He‚Äôs worked out with the Utah Jazz, Washington Wizards, Charlo

item.title: Hollywood stars and Wall Streeters pursuing Celebrity ‚Ä¶
item.description: The hunt for Knicks tickets has created a cutthroat Hunger Games‚Äìlike competition during this week‚Äôs NBA Finals. But Hollywood stars and Wall Streeters pursuing Celebrity Row seats at Madison Square Garden should be forewarned: There are some unofficial rules to scoring the ultimate in luxury, courtside seats, say sources. As the Knicks and Spurs face off at the World‚Äôs Most Famous Arena, they‚Äôll be flanked by a few dozen camera-friendly seats that have become known for hosting A-listers like Ti

item.title: The Garden is famous for the care and feeding of ‚Ä¶
item.description: The Garden is famous for the care and feeding of celebrity clientele. But don‚Äôt take its largesse for granted. If you make it to Celebrity Row, you‚Äôre expected to mostly remain in your seat‚Äîwithin camera range‚Äîuntil the end of the game. Supermodels Emily Ratajkowski and Irina Shayk committed the cardinal sin of leaving their comped seats early during a blowout. Guess what? The Knicks stormed back to beat the Heat. When Ratajkowski asked for free Rangers tickets later, the Garden turned her down

item.title: The Garden is not interested in just any celebrity. ‚Ä¶
item.description: The Garden is not interested in just any celebrity. They want people like Ben Stiller and Chris Rock, who‚Äôve demonstrated their loyalty to the Knicks. As former MSG executive Chris Weiller told The New York Times: ‚ÄúHow deep a fan you are has a lot to do with it.‚Äù Most Celebrity Row seats are controlled by the Garden. But not all. The NBA gets an allotment. Big Hollywood studios, talent agencies, and blue-chip sponsors buy some of their own.

item.title: Mikal Bridges after 'terrible' Game 3: 'I know personally I've got to be better'
item.description: Fred Katz: Mikal Bridges: "Personally, I think I played a terrible game. ... And I know personally I've got to be better. ... You can't sulk about last game. You gotta learn from it."

item.title: The NYPD took more than 20 people into custody near a ‚Ä¶
item.description: The NYPD took more than 20 people into custody near a Knicks watch party in Bryant Park Monday night, and several officers were injured. Police officials said about 7,000 people came to the Game 3 of the NBA Finals watch party, but it was the crowd outside of it that got violent.

item.title: Out of the 21 people taken into custody, eight were ‚Ä¶
item.description: Out of the 21 people taken into custody, eight were arrested and charged, while the others were released with criminal court summonses. Two people were charged with assaulting an officer, the NYPD said. Five members of the department were injured, according to police.

item.title: Informed of videos shared on social media showing fans ‚Ä¶
item.description: Victor Wembanyama was among players on both the Spurs and Knicks to condemn the videos permeating social media of apparent New York fans physically attacking San Antonio supporters. Informed of videos shared on social media showing fans attacking Spurs fans on the streets and ripping off their jerseys, Wembanyama denounced those actions. "My thoughts of course [are] that we can't forget it's a game," he said. "We're just playing a game out there. I am all for passion, but [with] the respect of e

item.title: "The game is built off of respect and passion," Knicks ‚Ä¶
item.description: "The game is built off of respect and passion," Knicks center Karl-Anthony Towns said. "We want everyone to respect each other. We want everyone to enjoy basketball at its purest state. It's the NBA Finals. There's no better place to watch basketball. Leave the physicality to everyone on the court" A Brooklyn native, Spurs forward Julian Champagnie pointed out that he doesn't "feel any threat" walking around Midtown Manhattan during San Antonio's time off the court.

item.title: Julian Champagnie on the atmosphere at Madison Square ‚Ä¶
item.description: Tom Orsborn: NYC native Julian Champagnie on the atmosphere at Madison Square Garden: "The initial reaction of just running out there, seeing all the celebrities, the fans, how loud it was, the music, it was definitely a surreal feeling that I'll carry for life. ... It was pretty cool."

item.title: LeBron James on Spurs: 'They're a team that doesn't have ego'
item.description: LeBron James: "San Antonio, they're a team that doesn't have ego. They have their personal beliefs on what they can bring to the table. We all do, we're all competitors, but that team is egoless. It's big time to see Josh (Hart) kind of growing to the player that he's in. We always talk about Alex Caruso a lot. It's those certain guys that you can plug into any franchise, any team, and they're going to win because those guys just make winning plays."

item.title: It seemed like Brunson was more so worried about the ‚Ä¶
item.description: DeMarcus Cousins: "Last night, I didn't like it. I feel like they switched up their style of play as far as allowing the offense to flow through KAT. It seemed like Brunson was more so worried about the headline, worried about being maybe the Finals MVP. That's what I saw in the moment. It didn't seem like it was the right form of basketball last night and I felt it was a lot of forced offense."

item.title: NBA rules no additional calls for Victor Wembanyama's shove on Jalen Brunson
item.description: Marc Stein: The NBA says there will be no additional sanctions against San Antonio's Victor Wembanyama for his first-quarter shove of Jalen Brunson in Game 3. The play is not being upgraded to a flagrant foul ... meaning Wembanyama remains on two flagrant foul points this postseason.

item.title: Jeremy Lin on Jalen Brunson: 'I don't think enough people are talking about him giving up $113 million'
item.description: Lin praised Brunson for igniting the Knicks‚Äô fanbase, saying the man known as ‚ÄúThe King of New York‚Äù is finally showing the world what New Yorkers have known all along. ‚ÄúHe is starting to get [the attention] now and definitely deserves it,‚Äù Lin told The California Post. ‚ÄúI don‚Äôt think enough people are talking about him giving up $113 million to build a team full of players that want to win. I think that speaks volumes. That‚Äôs also historic. It‚Äôs not talked about enough. Now he‚Äôs starting to get

item.title: We'll win this series on the court (even if the refs ‚Ä¶
item.description: Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani: Couldn't agree more. We'll win this series on the court (even if the refs refuse to call a flagrant on Wemby), not by targeting, harassing, or attacking Spurs fans. Knicks in 5.

item.title: Mike Brown on Karl-Anthony Towns' lack of touches: I got to do a better job of getting him more involved
item.description: So it put Towns is back into focus. "It's extremely important that he's getting touches, that he's involved, not just in the fourth quarter but obviously throughout the ballgame," Knicks coach Mike Brown said Tuesday at practice. "I've got to continue trying to do a better job of getting him involved throughout the course of the game, as well as late."

item.title: "Everybody is disappointed that we didn't go out and ‚Ä¶
item.description: "We have a veteran group. Nobody is quote-unquote 'panicking' or anything like that," Brown said. "Everybody is disappointed that we didn't go out and execute, and play to what we feel our standard is. That's not taking anything away from San Antonio, but we feel like we can play a lot better than what we did."

item.title: The initial reaction of just running out there, seeing ‚Ä¶
item.description: During the Spurs media availability on Tuesday afternoon, he was asked, ‚ÄúAfter Game 7 in OKC, you were asked about returning back to New York. Now in the Finals, now that you‚Äôve experienced that, could you share what it was like for you to play last night?‚Äù ‚ÄúYeah, it was pretty cool. Obviously we had a game to win, and that was the most important thing, especially being down 0-2. The initial reaction of just running out there, seeing all the celebrities, the fans, how loud it was, the music, it

item.title: ‚ÄúI think we contributed to the confusion by not being ‚Ä¶
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs attempted to make a substitution with just over five minutes left in Game 3 at Madison Square Garden in New York, N.Y. Initially, the substitution did not take place, but officials abruptly whistled the play dead just after the New York Knicks had inbounded the ball to try to figure out what was going on. NBA head of officiating Monty McCutchen felt that the game officials were not authoritative enough and got themselves into a mess as a result. ‚ÄúYou have to run the game wi

item.title: Attendance‚Ä¶The actions of the Mayor and Police ‚Ä¶
item.description: Ian Begley: Statement from MSG Sports: ‚ÄúWe have been told that the NYPD will once again implement the same 'frozen zone' restrictions for Game 4, which were supposedly to thwart any threats related to the President's attendance‚Ä¶The actions of the Mayor and Police Commissioner, supposedly in support of the Knicks and their fans, are difficult to understand.‚Äù Full statement below. Police previously said prior to Game 3 that the area around MSG was shut down due to President‚Äôs visit. This action wo

item.title: The New Yorks Knicks 105-104 victory over the San ‚Ä¶
item.description: The New Yorks Knicks 105-104 victory over the San Antonio Spurs on June 5 was ABC‚Äôs most-watched NBA Finals Game 2 since 2018 ‚Äì according to Nielsen Big Data + Panel. Game 2 generated an average audience of 16.43M viewers and peaked with 19.42M viewers at 11:15 p.m. ET. Viewership was up 88% from last year‚Äôs Game 2.

item.title: Those first 2 games really hurt, and I feel like it ‚Ä¶
item.description: Ballislife.com: Stephon Castle on if he's expecting to win Game 4 at MSG: "Yeah, definitely. I expect us to win the next three, but we're just taking it game by game, quarter by quarter, and trying to walk this thing down. Those first 2 games really hurt, and I feel like it was an eye-opener for us. We don't want it to be that way. We want to punch first."

item.title: He recalled one game where the team was vying for a ‚Ä¶
item.description: When Knicks point guard Jose Alvarado collided with former mayor Mike Bloomberg while chasing a loose ball in Game 3 ‚Äî and then made sure the 83-year-old was OK ‚Äî it was no surprise to those who grew up coaching the determined-but-kind kid from the Brooklyn projects. ‚ÄúHe fought and he fought and he fought,‚Äù Dan Klores said of a teenage Alvarado‚Äôs focus and grit playing for him in the New York RENS youth league. He recalled one game where the team was vying for a championship. A young Alvarado do

item.title: ‚ÄúHe says to me, ‚ÄòDan, I‚Äôm playing.‚Äô I said, ‚ÄòJose, are ‚Ä¶
item.description: Alvarado was taken away on a stretcher and the team lost the game. But, they still had a chance at the championship and Alvarado insisted on playing again. ‚ÄúHe says to me, ‚ÄòDan, I‚Äôm playing.‚Äô I said, ‚ÄòJose, are you kidding me? You can‚Äôt play.‚Äô His father comes over to me, Jose Sr., he says, ‚ÄòCoach that‚Äôs my son. He‚Äôs playing,'‚Äù Klores said. ‚ÄúWe [went on to] win the game.‚Äù

item.title: Spurs have clean injury report ahead of Game 4
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Spurs have clean injury report ahead of Game 4.

item.title: Jose Alvarado on Victor Wembanyama tossing Jalen Brunson to floor: That's something that they gotta look at, that'll be the last one
item.description: In the first quarter of the Spurs‚Äô victory at the Garden, Wembanyama tossed Jalen Brunson to the floor. No foul was called. ‚ÄúI think that‚Äôs not basketball,‚Äù Jose Alvarado, the feisty reserve guard from Brooklyn, said on Tuesday. ‚ÄúThat‚Äôs something that they gotta look at. But he got away with one. That‚Äôll be the last one.‚Äù

item.title: Stephon Castle on Game 4: 'I expect us to win the next three'
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Stephon Castle on if he expects to win game 4 in Madison Square Garden ‚ÄúI expect us to win the next three‚Äù

item.title: Rick Brunson on defending Jalen Brunson during scuffle with De'Aaron Fox: 'I'm always gonna be a father first'
item.description: ‚ÄúI‚Äôm always gonna be a father first,‚Äù Rick said on ‚ÄúGood Morning America‚Äù Monday during a joint interview with Jalen. ‚ÄúBut at the end of the day, if there was any other player, I‚Äôd do the same.‚Äù For Rick, the flashpoint was just another reminder of the emotional volatility that comes with watching his son operate under the brightest lights in basketball ‚Äî something he says still feels is surreal. ‚ÄúIt‚Äôs crazy to me. I never envisioned, you know, to this level,‚Äù he said of Jalen‚Äôs rise.

item.title: Bucks monitoring Knicks' Finals run before revisiting Giannis Antetokounmpo trade talks?
item.description: There is an increasing belief leaguewide that they would prefer to wait until after the NBA Finals before completing a deal to see if the Knicks end up squandering a 2-0 series lead. Might the Knicks then decide to re-enter the Giannis Trade Sweepstakes in response to the disappointment of letting the title slip away?

item.title: If there's a clear miss, then there's a possibility ‚Ä¶
item.description: Ian Begley: In the NBA Today interview, McCutchen acknowledged that Wembanyama committed a foul and explained the standard involved in elevating a call: ‚ÄúEvery game is reviewed by the league office postgame, the entirety of the game, and we're in the process of doing that today. Most certainly, it's important to note, though, as we do that review, that there's a real deference, just like in our replay center, Malika, that we want a clear and conclusive standard to upgrade something, because we d

item.title: Madison Square Garden and the New York Knicks are mad ‚Ä¶
item.description: Madison Square Garden and the New York Knicks are mad at the New York Police Department and Mayor Zohran Mamdani for not letting them throw a big enough party for Game 4 of the NBA Finals. In a statement released Tuesday, the team called NYPD commissioner Jessica Tisch and Mamdani the city‚Äôs ‚Äúbiggest party poopers‚Äù for putting security restrictions around the Garden for Wednesday night‚Äôs contest against the San Antonio Spurs. While the city has approved a watch party, the venue is complaining th

item.title: "I didn‚Äôt know that‚Ä¶ We‚Äôre just playing a game out ‚Ä¶
item.description: Victor Wembanyama when asked about reports of Knicks fans attacking Spurs fans in New York after Game 3: "I didn‚Äôt know that‚Ä¶ We‚Äôre just playing a game out there. And I‚Äôm all for passion, but to the respect of each other. It‚Äôs unacceptable."

item.title: What did you think of the reception you got from ‚Ä¶
item.description: Aaron Rupar: Q: What did you think of the reception you got from Knicks fans tonight? TRUMP: I thought great. I thought it was amazing, actually. It was I think mostly cheers. (Trump was loudly booed)

item.title: I was a Michael Jordan fan. Michael Jordan is my ‚Ä¶
item.description: JR Smith: "I did not grow up a Knicks fan. I hated the Knicks. I did not like Patrick Ewing. I did not like John Starks. I didn't like anything about the Knicks. I was a Bulls fan. I was a Michael Jordan fan. Michael Jordan is my GOAT."

item.title: "While I was here, we brought in a person who lied to ‚Ä¶
item.description: JR Smith on being betrayed by a teammate in New York: "While I was here, we brought in a person who lied to me. Lied to me to my face multiple times, told things that I told him in secret publicly. And I felt some type of way about it, obviously, which I think most people would. I was really treated like a pawn onto this bigger chess game that they were playing and I didn't appreciate that."

I think basically she at that time was filming her show, which is, I don't know if it's Keeping Up with the Kardashians or whatever show was happening back then, but I know that she was looking for kind of like a New York athlete to kind of be on this show essentially."

item.title: His NBA Finals MVP odds also fell off a cliff, as he ‚Ä¶
item.description: Karl-Anthony Towns was apparently the biggest loser of Game 3 for the Knicks. Towns scored just 11 points in 38 minutes with eight rebounds and just one assist, the lowest total he‚Äôs had since Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Finals against the Cavaliers. His NBA Finals MVP odds also fell off a cliff, as he was +185 to win Finals MVP after Game 2, but those odds have grown to +550 on FanDuel at the time of writing.

So he's just been a great dude and that's the head of New York, you know what I'm saying?"

I was in consistent pain, and I turned to pills. Took a lot of pills, took a lot of painkillers, and I got into a bad situation. I let this person know that. I was also going through a breakup. item.description: In Fort Greene, Brooklyn, a towering broadcast was projected on the side of a building as people watched from the street and on nearby rooftops. Outside the Sugar Hill Creamery in Harlem, kids devoured ice cream as the game was illuminated on pieces of poster board tied to scaffolding. Inmates at Rikers Island with records of good behavior were granted a viewing. The games are shown in barbershops, laundromats, and at least one gas station in the West Village. item.description: As the Knicks vie for their first NBA title since 1973, law and finance professionals tell Front Office Sports that the historic playoff run has provided some rare moments of relief from New York‚Äôs famously relentless work culture. Throughout the postseason‚Äîwhich began for the Knicks on April 18‚Äîsources say their inboxes have gotten leaner and some requests have less urgency. item.title: Game 3 of the 2026 NBA Finals between the San Antonio ‚Ä¶
item.description: NBA Communications: Game 3 of the 2026 NBA Finals between the San Antonio Spurs and New York Knicks was the most-watched NBA Finals Game 3 in 28 years (since Bulls/Jazz in 1998), averaging 23.8 million viewers on ABC and ESPN. The audience peaked with 26.3 million viewers at 11:15 p.m. ET. Game 4 tips off tonight at 8:30 p.m. ET on ABC.

item.title: Jalen Brunson on some Knicks' fans being disrespectful: 'I love our fans, but we've got to be better'
item.description: Speaking to NBC News' Jesse Kirsch, Brunson urged Knicks fans to be more disciplined and decent, as New York's celebration of making it back to the Finals stage for the first time in 27 years has been marred by violence and several arrests. "You never want to be disrespectful. Throwing stuff and saying things that are not part of the game is considered disrespectful," the Knicks superstar said. "I love our fans, but we've got to be better."
